acceptance:
1. Add `TestExecutionCleanupManager_ApplyReapsOnlyStaleAttemptProcessGroups` using injected scanner/killer fakes where needed, proving `--apply` terminates only stale DDx-owned attempt process groups.
2. Add `TestExecutionCleanupManager_ApplyPreservesLiveAndForeignProcessGroups` proving live run-state, live worker sidecar, active registered worktree, other-project processes, and uncertain candidates are preserved with operator-visible warnings when appropriate.
3. Add `TestExecutionCleanupManager_ApplyTerminatesGracefullyThenBoundedKill` or an equivalent assertion proving the killer uses graceful terminate before bounded kill when a process group remains alive.
4. Existing process-reaper tests remain green: `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'OrphanHarness|Executor|DockerCloneBackend_ProcessGroupCleanup' -count=1` passes.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'ExecutionCleanupManager_Apply.*Process|ProcessReap|AttemptDescendant|OrphanHarness|Executor|DockerCloneBackend_ProcessGroupCleanup' -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
`ddx cleanup --apply` can delete stale DDx execution files, but it cannot terminate stale DDx-owned attempt-descendant process groups that are provably orphaned. Heavy leftover processes can continue burning CPU or holding ports after their original attempt is gone.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:230-527` applies file cleanup for stale temp dirs, worktrees, scratch roots, stale run-state files, evidence dirs, logs, worker dirs, and refs, but contains no safe process-group termination path.
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:110-138` has no process reaping counts or warnings in `ExecutionCleanupSummary`, so apply mode cannot report terminated, preserved, blocked, or unsafe process groups.
- `cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper.go:82-100` and `cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper.go:155-168` intentionally gate startup harness reaping on top-level harness names and dead bead lease owners; that does not cover descendant process groups whose harness root has already exited.
- `cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:10-29` sets process group and parent-death attributes for normal harness children, which cleanup can use only when stale ownership and liveness evidence prove the group is DDx-owned and safe to reap.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend execution cleanup apply mode with an injectable process-group killer and conservative stale ownership checks. Apply may terminate only process groups associated with the current project's DDx execution root where run-state cleanup liveness is expired or missing, the recorded owner PID is gone, the worktree is under the current project execution root, and no live worker sidecar or active registered worktree owns it. Preserve live run-state, live worker sidecar, active registered worktree, other-project processes, and any uncertain candidate. Prefer graceful terminate followed by bounded kill where feasible. Surface blocked warnings instead of silently deleting files when a process is detected but cannot be killed safely.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go` and adjacent new platform-specific process cleanup files/tests.
- `cli/internal/workerstatus/*` or `cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper*` only for reused process-scanning helpers, not broad rewrites.
- `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md` and `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md` only if this child changes the documented cleanup contract.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not edit Niflheim.
- Do not duplicate `ddx-e0de6369`; that bead owns `.ddx/harness-sessions/` scratch ignore/prune behavior.
- Do not duplicate `ddx-bbea954f`; that bead owns dirty timeout worktree edit preservation before cleanup deletion.
- Do not replace the existing parent-death/process-group fixes from `ddx-0d7d5f0e`, `ddx-3113d524`, or `ddx-418c3849`.
- Do not implement a general-purpose port scanner or host process manager. Port conflict recovery here comes only from safely reaping stale DDx-owned attempt process groups.
- Do not kill processes outside the current project's DDx execution root, processes tied to live run-state/worker liveness, active registered worktrees, or non-DDx user workloads.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS
- `spec:FEAT-010`
- `spec:SD-025`

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f` (multi-worker reliability and Niflheim local gates).
Deps: none.

description:
PROBLEM
The internal/agent package gate reaches a host-global DDx scratch tree through the work-loop cleanup fixture. On 2026-07-13, TestWorkCleanup_RunsAfterSetupFailureBeforeNextClaim performed about 431 MB of physical reads and exceeded 120 seconds in isolation, making ddx work test latency depend on accumulated host state.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_cleanup_test.go:269-288 constructs a production NewExecutionCleanupManager rooted at t.TempDir but leaves TempRoot and ScratchRoots unset. cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:830-843 then derives the shared cache parent and legacy process temp directory, and cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:620-747 recursively measures old matching scratch directories.

PROPOSED FIX
Give the work-loop cleanup fixture explicit temp and scratch roots beneath its own t.TempDir. Add a sentinel test proving cleanup cannot enumerate, measure, or remove a scratch root outside that fixture. Preserve the setup-failure-before-next-claim behavior under test.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_cleanup_test.go
- The nearest test helper in that file if needed

NON-SCOPE
Do not alter production cleanup behavior, retention policy, or cleanup assertions. Do not audit other ExecutionCleanupManager fixtures; ddx-34113f35 owns that follow-up. Do not skip tests or weaken timeouts.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-e6abf4a6. Deps: none.

acceptance:
1. TestWorkerStatusReportsFDExhaustionForMissingDesiredWorker proves status output shows a desired worker missing because its last terminal event was fd exhaustion.
2. TestWorkerStatusJSONIncludesFDExhaustionForMissingDesiredWorker proves the JSON status includes desired_count, missing worker count, and fd-exhaustion diagnosis fields.
3. TestWorkStatusAllProjectsRetainsLiveWorkerListing proves the new diagnostic does not hide or regress existing live-worker rows in `ddx work status --all-projects`.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./cmd/... -run "TestWorkerStatusReportsFDExhaustionForMissingDesiredWorker|TestWorkerStatusJSONIncludesFDExhaustionForMissingDesiredWorker|TestWorkStatusAllProjectsRetainsLiveWorkerListing" passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... ./cmd/... passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
A project can have `desired_count=1` with no live worker after the last server-managed worker exited from fd exhaustion. `ddx work status --all-projects` only reports live workers, while `ddx worker status` reports desired state but not why the desired worker is missing. Operators cannot see that the last terminal worker exited due to fd exhaustion.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/work_status.go:80-115 builds status from live worker scans and active work only.
cli/cmd/work_status.go:215-221 prints `No live ddx workers...` when no workers are present, with no desired-state or last-terminal reason.
cli/cmd/worker.go:121-158 reads `.ddx/workers/desired.json` and prints desired_count/restart state, but does not consult terminal worker history or last failure reason.
cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor.go:351-389 records terminal worker history for restart accounting, but that diagnosis is not exposed in the operator-facing desired-worker status path.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an operator-visible diagnostic to the worker status surface for desired workers that are missing because their last terminal event/result was fd exhaustion. Include the diagnosis in text and JSON output, preserving existing live-worker output. Use the structured fd-exhaustion fields emitted by the agent/server path rather than brittle string matching where possible.

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken byte/inode resource guards. Do not change bead claim semantics. Do not remove the existing supervisor stale-operator-attention fixes tracked by ddx-b17cad6d and ddx-4d92f2cf. Do not implement the low-level EMFILE classifier in this bead except as needed by tests/fixtures.

acceptance:
1. `ddx init --global --silent` exits 0 and creates ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/ with the bundled library content.
2. `ddx init --global` is idempotent: running twice does not error and does not destroy local-only files under global/.
3. TestInitGlobal_PopulatesXDGGlobalDir asserts the directory structure post-install matches what convention mode expects.
4. TestInitGlobal_IsIdempotent asserts a second run does not error and re-syncs the plugin content.
5. TestConventionMode_ReadsGlobalSkillsBeforeHardcoded asserts that with --global installed, convention mode picks up the global plugin instead of the hardcoded one.
6. ddx doctor's "Global Install" check exists and reports the install path + status.
7. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/config/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Convention mode (per ddx-ad338ec3) bootstraps an XDG project dir but has no source for skills, personas, or the default ddx plugin. Either every project re-downloads the plugin on first use, or the user must have a global install. The latter is the better answer: install once per machine, all convention-mode projects share.

ROOT CAUSE
There is no per-user install path today. `ddx init` only writes per-project state. Skills/personas live in the project's .ddx/plugins/ (in-tree) or are missing entirely (convention).

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add `ddx init --global` flag (and corresponding code path in cli/cmd/init.go). When set:
   - Resolve target path: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ (defaults to ~/.local/share/ddx/global/).
   - Install the default ddx plugin (library/ contents) into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/.
   - Write a default ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults (library URL, branch, etc.).
   - Idempotent: re-running upgrades to a newer plugin version but does not destroy operator state.

2. Convention mode reads ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ as the second precedence tier (in-tree > global > hardcoded). If --global has never been run, fall back to baked-in hardcoded defaults — convention mode still works without it; --global is an optimization for users who want offline-cacheable plugin content.

3. Document the install path in ddx doctor: a new check "Global Install" reports whether ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ exists and points operators to `ddx init --global` if it does not.

4. Do NOT remove the existing per-project plugin install (in-tree mode at .ddx/plugins/<name>/). Both coexist.

NON-SCOPE
- In-project ddx init manifest write (E4 bead).
- Migration from legacy ~/.config/ddx/ or ~/.ddx/ if such exist on disk (none expected; today there is no global install).
- Plugin update / upgrade flow for the global install (use existing `ddx update` mechanism; verify it works against the new path).

DEPS
ddx-564ea1cf (E1) — resolver must exist.
ddx-ad338ec3 (E2) — convention mode reads from this path.
notes: Superseded by epic restructure into 3 consolidated beads aligned to the two principles: (1) bulletproof ddx home resolution, (2) centralized vs in-project skills/personas. New beads pending.
title: config: ddx init --global installs user-tier skills/personas to XDG global

description:
PROBLEM
Operators need one doctor/status surface for the worker self-healing invariants rather than discovering failures through ps, logs, or manual tracker inspection.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
ddx doctor already has housekeeping checks in cli/cmd/doctor_housekeeping_test.go:27-60, but it does not report the final self-healing invariants from this plan: stale terminal suppression, PID/PGID liveness mismatch, runtime JSONL union/lock coverage, unresolved preserved-review gate, and resource pressure.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend doctor/status checks to report these conditions using the final APIs from the preceding beads. In dry-run mode report findings and remediation. In --apply mode only perform already-supported housekeeping cleanup; do not mutate bead lifecycle or code.

NON-SCOPE
Do not start/stop workers from doctor. Do not create beads from doctor.
title: Report worker self-healing risks in doctor

3. The reconciliation notes cite `TP-002-server-web-ui.md:340-351,353-363`, `beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts:210-268`, `spec-editing.spec.ts:204-256`, and `federation-2node.spec.ts:219-307`.

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx-359e812b` is still too broad for what remains. `docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:340-351,353-363` keeps bead, spec, and document writes planned, but the current Playwright suite already covers the mocked forwarded paths in `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts:210-268` and `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/spec-editing.spec.ts:204-256`, while `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts:219-307` already covers the read-federation shell around the owner-write entry points.

ROOT CAUSE
The stale bead conflates three concerns: hub/spoke federation reads, mocked UI write-forwarding, and the live owner-targeted persistence gap. FEAT-026 says forwarding is owner-targeted and never broadcast (`docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-026-federation.md:22-29,160-177`), so the bead should only track the missing live bead/document write persistence and refusal behavior, not the already-covered smoke paths.

PROPOSED FIX
- Rewrite or supersede `ddx-359e812b` so it narrows to live owner-targeted bead create/edit and documentWrite persistence.
- Keep the ts-net guard as a separate child if it is still load-bearing.
- Add or update the missing owner-write tests as `TestFederatedBeadWrite_ForwardsToOwner`, `TestFederatedDocumentWrite_ForwardsToOwner`, `TestFederatedWrite_RejectsOfflineSpoke`, and `TestFederatedWrite_IdempotentRequestID`.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add new federation read smoke.
- Do not add screenshot assertions.
- Do not implement managed-node outbound control from ADR-028.
- Do not change the mocked Playwright smoke paths unless they are being split out of this bead.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: `ddx-a038a090`. Upstream stale bead context: `ddx-28f3cf37`.

No further edit to ddx-359e812b is needed for AC1-AC3. AC4's Go leg passes: `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/graphql/... ./internal/federation/...` -> ok for both packages. AC4's Playwright leg does not pass and cannot be made to pass within this bead's NON-SCOPE. `bunx playwright test e2e/beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts e2e/spec-editing.spec.ts` fails 8 of 10 tests, reproducibly across two runs. Both spec files self-document this as expected, pre-existing, unfinished-UI state, not a regression: - beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts:8-9: "Most of these tests MUST FAIL until the corresponding queue-mutation UI is implemented in the server operator workbench." - spec-editing.spec.ts:8-9: "These tests MUST FAIL until the full spec-editing UI with path confinement, stale-write detection, federation forwarding, and graph refresh is implemented." Root causes are unrelated to federation write-forwarding and this bead's reconciliation scope: - BeadForm.svelte's BeadCreate/BeadUpdate mutation input never includes `projectId` (cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/components/BeadForm.svelte:99-121), so "creates a bead in the selected project only" and "forwards spoke bead writes from the hub" fail on `toMatchObject({ projectId: ... })`. - The bead detail route's Edit/lifecycle controls are not reachable the way the specs probe, so "edits bead fields...", "runs lifecycle actions...", and the equivalent spec-editing saves/stale/refresh tests time out waiting for controls. Implementing this UI wiring is real feature work, not a tracker/description narrowing chore, and is explicitly out of this bead's NON-SCOPE ("Do not change the mocked Playwright smoke paths unless they are being split out of this bead"). The Go-level federation write-forwarding contract these specs gesture at is already tracked separately by open beads ddx-3f3e1cbe ("federation: prove live owner-targeted writes route to spoke") and ddx-20e7ebf0 ("federation: prove forwarded write idempotency"). Needs an operator decision: either drop/relax the Playwright leg of AC4 on ddx-19f9c9c0 (since it exercises pre-existing, self-documented-red UI gaps unrelated to this bead's actual deliverable, which is already complete), or file a new bug bead for the BeadForm.svelte projectId/edit-control gaps so AC4 has something concrete to depend on.

acceptance:
1. TestUsageAggregationNeverEstimatesCostFromModelIdentity gives identical missing-cost rows different model/harness/provider identities and proves every result remains unknown with equal totals.
2. TestProcessMetricsNeverEstimatesCostFromModelIdentity proves process and bead reports classify absent cost as unknown and never call a local model-price table.
3. TestSessionIndexUsesOnlyFizeauReportedBilling table-tests fixed, per_token, subscription, empty, and unfamiliar public Fizeau Billing values; only known enums map to presentation buckets and all route identities produce identical results.
4. TestLegacySessionReindexPreservesUnknownCostAndBilling proves historical rows without explicit presence stay unknown rather than receiving inferred model cost or billing.
5. TestGraphQLCostSummaryPreservesUnknownFizeauFacts proves session rows and summaries do not synthesize cash, subscription-equivalent, or local cost when Fizeau omitted billing/cost evidence.
6. TestEvidenceCapsResolveOnlyByDDXRole proves global plus implementer/reviewer/lifecycle overrides work and changing Harness, Provider, or Model cannot change any cap.
7. TestLoadConfigHardErrorsOnHarnessKeyedEvidenceCaps proves evidence_caps.per_harness returns a typed actionable migration error.
8. TestProviderDisplayNameUsesFizeauInventoryVerbatim proves arbitrary current and future Fizeau harness names are displayed without a DDx lookup table.
9. rg -n 'EstimateCost|billingModeFor|BillingModeFor|isSubscriptionHarnessName|harnessDisplayName|per_harness|PerHarness' cli --glob '*.go' --glob '*.json' returns only migration/rejection fixtures and no production inference path.
10. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/processmetrics/... ./internal/config/... ./internal/evidence/... ./internal/server/... -run 'Test(UsageAggregationNeverEstimatesCostFromModelIdentity|ProcessMetricsNeverEstimatesCostFromModelIdentity|SessionIndexUsesOnlyFizeauReportedBilling|LegacySessionReindexPreservesUnknownCostAndBilling|GraphQLCostSummaryPreservesUnknownFizeauFacts|EvidenceCapsResolveOnlyByDDXRole|LoadConfigHardErrorsOnHarnessKeyedEvidenceCaps|ProviderDisplayNameUsesFizeauInventoryVerbatim)' -count=1 -timeout 300s passes.
11. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/processmetrics/... ./internal/config/... ./internal/evidence/... ./internal/server/... -count=1 -timeout 600s passes.
12. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
DDx still infers price, billing category, subscription status, display identity, and evidence limits from concrete model/harness/provider names. These facts become stale as Fizeau changes routes and cause unknown cost to be presented as an estimate or fabricated paid/subscription/local category. Concrete route identity also changes prompt-size policy through per-harness evidence caps.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/pricing.go:3-34 contains a DDx model-price table and EstimateCost.
- cli/cmd/usage_support.go:54-72 estimates missing session cost from model identity; lines 106-139 infer subscription/value basis from Claude/Codex names.
- cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:1274-1323 turns missing cost into model-derived estimated cost.
- cli/internal/agent/billing_mode.go:15-49 maps harness, surface, provider, and endpoint identity to paid/subscription/local.
- cli/internal/agent/session_index.go:123-128, 194-215, and 269-293 derive or backfill billing from route identity.
- cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:721-745 and 778-792 repeat the identity-derived billing fallback in summaries and session rows.
- cli/internal/config/types.go:191-223, cli/internal/config/evidence_caps.go:5-30, cli/internal/evidence/caps.go:64-77, and cli/internal/config/resolved.go:112 select evidence limits by concrete harness name.
- cli/internal/server/providers.go:691-714 owns a concrete harness display-name registry.

PROPOSED FIX
Delete the DDx Pricing table and EstimateCost. Usage and process metrics aggregate only Fizeau-reported cost with explicit presence evidence; missing cost remains unknown and is never estimated from Model. Capture the selected Fizeau routing-decision candidate public Billing enum as audit evidence and map only that enum to DDx presentation buckets; empty or unfamiliar values remain unknown and identity is never consulted. Preserve explicit cost presence through Result and SessionIndexEntry. Legacy rows without presence remain unknown during reindex. Remove Claude/Codex subscription inference. Replace evidence_caps.per_harness with evidence_caps.per_role keyed only by DDx semantic roles implementer, reviewer, and lifecycle; hard-error on legacy per_harness. Remove harnessDisplayName and display Fizeau inventory Name verbatim. Fizeau-reported cost, billing, usage, and route facts remain read-only evidence and never route-selection inputs.

NON-SCOPE
Do not add a replacement price catalog, infer that an unproven numeric zero means free, remove generic operator budget/cost caps, change Fizeau pricing/routing, or use billing/cost for retry, admission, escalation, review grading, or close policy.

PARENT
ddx-b8cacbeb.

DEPS
ddx-e2c3453b removes endpoint billing/default synthesis first so this bead has one authoritative Fizeau input path.

acceptance:
1. TestImporter_Apply_WritesToPostgres covers importing active plus archive JSONL beads into the Axon-backed store and asserts bead and inline-event counts.
2. TestImporter_Apply_PreservesArchiveState covers archived source rows remaining distinguishable from active source rows after import through the Axon-backed Store read surface.
3. TestImporter_Apply_WritesInlineEvents covers inline events from imported source rows being persisted with the imported bead records.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -run 'TestImporter_Apply_WritesToPostgres|TestImporter_Apply_PreservesArchiveState|TestImporter_Apply_WritesInlineEvents' passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./... passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The Axon importer apply path is too broad to execute as one bead. This child scopes the core persistence slice authorized by governing artifact TD-027: writing prepared active/archive JSONL bead rows and inline events into the Axon-backed Store through the existing store write surface.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
Production AxonStore migration needs a real importer behind Migrator.MigrateToAxon, but the migration boundary is only the admin interface at cli/internal/bead/migrator.go:14 and its store delegate at cli/internal/bead/migrator.go:53. The importer implementation belongs at cli/internal/bead/import_postgres.go:61, where the target Store and source .ddx directory can be combined. Today the importer does not persist the parsed JSONL corpus into the Axon-backed Store, so .ddx/beads.jsonl and .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl corpora never become target bead records.

PROPOSED FIX
Complete cli/internal/bead/import_postgres.go so importJSONLCorpusToAxon takes the prepared source corpus, writes each bead through the Axon-backed Store write surface, preserves active/archive state required by the store model, and writes inline events associated with imported beads. Return importer counts for bead writes and inline-event writes so callers can report the migration result.

NON-SCOPE
- Schema-versioning migration ladder; deferred until first v1-to-v2 breaking change.
- Real-wire integration test against actual Postgres; separate WIRE bead.
- Continuous sync between JSONL and Postgres.
- CLI flag/output work beyond what is needed to call the existing Migrator surface; sibling child owns CLI UX coverage.
- Event sidecar copy and read-back drift verification beyond preserving inline events needed for apply-path tests; sibling child owns attachments and verification depth.
- Do not claim or edit parent bead ddx-53df5e2f tracker data manually; tracker mutations must go through ddx bead commands.
- Source-corpus limit and no-source behavior beyond consuming the importer source preparation contract; sibling child owns source loading.
- Re-run idempotency beyond avoiding duplicate writes within one prepared corpus; sibling child owns second-run semantics.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-3d96c6f9. Decomposes too-large bead ddx-53df5e2f. Dependencies: no deps.

title: Implement bead importer Axon write path for beads and inline events

acceptance:
1. TestSupervisorReconcile_LiveDiskOnlyAttemptSurvivesTicks removes or omits the manager handle for a real live managed-attempt fixture, runs at least three ReconcileAt ticks, and proves the process remains alive.
2. TestSupervisorReconcile_LiveDiskOnlyAttemptSurvivesTicks proves the fresh claim remains in_progress and supervisor reconciliation does not create a replacement duplicate for that attempt.
3. TestSupervisorReconcile_LiveDiskOnlyAttemptSurvivesTicks proves preservation emits or observes a structured supervisor/lifecycle diagnostic containing worker ID, PID, bead ID, and preservation reason.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run "TestSupervisorReconcile_LiveDiskOnlyAttemptSurvivesTicks" passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
After restart, re-exec, or reconciliation, the server may have a live managed worker represented only by a disk record and no in-memory manager handle. Such a worker should survive supervisor ticks when its same-machine lease and heartbeat are fresh, and the supervisor must not start a replacement duplicate.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor.go:270-279 sends every snapshot stale ID through stopStaleDiskEntry. Because stopStaleDiskEntry currently cleans a live PID before checking whether staleDiskEntryCanReleaseClaim refuses release, a disk-only live worker can be killed during reconciliation. The existing TestSupervisorReconcile_FreshClaimingWorkerSurvivesTicks at cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor_test.go:882-962 keeps the live worker in the manager handle map and uses stale fixture PID 0, so it cannot detect a live PID represented only by a stale disk entry.

PROPOSED FIX
Adjust supervisor reconciliation so live same-machine disk-only managed attempts with fresh bead leases or heartbeats are preserved or adopted instead of stopped, and ensure the desired-count reconciliation does not create a replacement duplicate for the same live attempt. Cover the case with a real live managed-attempt fixture whose manager handle is removed or omitted before multiple ReconcileAt ticks.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change desired worker count, generic process cleanup, provider parent-death behavior, or manual/server coordination owned by ddx-635ae251. Do not weaken cleanup of dead PIDs or genuinely stale leases. Do not broaden adoption semantics beyond live same-machine disk-only managed attempts with fresh claim evidence.

PARENT
Split from root regression bead "server: preserve live managed PIDs during stale-record cleanup".

DEPS
None.

acceptance:
1. TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup proves StartExecuteLoop creates a server-managed drain worker with nonzero PID and PGID in WorkerRecord and persisted status.json.
2. TestManagedWorkerServerManagedFlagRunsWorkLoop proves the hidden ddx work --server-managed <worker-id> entry point reads the persisted spec and runs the same execute-loop path used by current server-managed workers.
3. TestManagedWorkerProcessBoundarySkipsExternalReportedWorkers proves process-boundary recording is applied only to server-owned work workers, not external reported workers or interactive Claude/Codex sessions.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run 'TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup|TestManagedWorkerServerManagedFlagRunsWorkLoop|TestManagedWorkerProcessBoundarySkipsExternalReportedWorkers' passes.
5. make install is run after the code change lands.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Server-managed drain workers are still started as in-process goroutines, so their WorkerRecord usually has PID zero and no PGID. The server cannot reliably target the ddx work process tree that later launches claude, codex, shell, or sleep descendants.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:123-127 records only PID and has no persisted PGID. cli/internal/server/workers.go:192 defines WorkerManager as managing in-process work workers as goroutines. cli/internal/server/workers.go:438 creates a cancelable context, and cli/internal/server/workers.go:461-462 starts drainProgress and runWorker goroutines instead of a server-owned OS process. cli/internal/server/workers.go:414-436 writes the initial WorkerRecord before any managed worker process boundary exists.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a server-managed subprocess launch path for StartExecuteLoop in cli/internal/server/workers.go:361-464. Persist the ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec as today, then start the current ddx binary as ddx work --server-managed <worker-id> or an equivalent hidden internal entry point in its own process group. Record nonzero PID and PGID on WorkerRecord and status.json. Keep the existing in-process path only where tests explicitly inject it, and do not make externally reported workers look server-owned.

NON-SCOPE
Do not kill external reported workers or interactive Claude/Codex sessions. Do not change bead claim semantics except releasing claims for the stopped/reaped managed worker. Do not implement UI controls.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT AND RELATIONSHIP
Parent bead: ddx-9d1af129. Governing artifact reference: plan:server-managed-workers. No explicit dependency IDs were provided by the parent bead.

acceptance:
1. TestTrackerStaleLockBreakSingleWinner uses coordinated cross-process contenders against one stale process-shared main-git lock and proves exactly one contender owns the guarded stale-to-tombstone disposal transition.
2. TestTrackerStaleLockBreakLoserNeverDeletesWinner forces the delayed-observer counterexample from an internal guard-stage barrier and proves the delayed contender cannot move, delete, or alter the fresh replacement pid, acquired_at, or owner_token bytes.
3. TestTrackerLockLeaseReleaseDoesNotDeleteReplacement age-supersedes a live lease, acquires and holds a replacement lease, then releases the old lease and proves token mismatch preserves the replacement metadata byte-for-byte.
4. TestTrackerStaleLockGuardCrashReleasesWithoutReplacingSidecar waits for a child signal after actual advisory-lock acquisition, kills and waits for that child, proves a successor acquires the same sidecar, and verifies os.SameFile before and after.
5. TestTrackerStaleLockGuardContentionRespectsPolicyDeadline holds the advisory guard in a child, uses a short LockRetryPolicy, and proves acquisition returns a TrackerLockTimeoutError within the bounded MaxElapsed tolerance without invoking the callback or mutating the canonical lock.
6. TestTrackerStaleLockMetadataPolicyPreserved proves valid dead-PID OR valid over-age acquired_at remains sufficient, while missing/malformed metadata without a valid stale criterion is preserved. Existing TestTrackerCommit_StaleLockRecovery, TestTrackerCommit_MalformedRegularFileLockRecovery, TestTrackerCommit_MalformedFreshRegularFileFailsFast, TestTrackerCommit_MalformedSymlinkLockFailsFast, and TestTrackerLock_StaleLockAfterPartialLocalLand remain green.
7. withTrackerLockPolicy uses a token-bearing lease and surfaces guarded release errors; withMainGitLock, WithMainGitLock, and withTrackerLock retain their callback signatures; no main-git-lock acquisition or release path directly removes the canonical pathname without the transition guard and token check.
8. TestTrackerLockRuntimePathsIgnoredWithoutGitignore proves the stable transition sidecar is excluded from synthesizeCommitExcludePathspecs, preDispatchCheckpointIgnoredPath, and checkoutSyncDeferralIgnoredPath even when .gitignore has no applicable rule; ordinary untracked implementation files remain visible.
9. Guard contention consumes the existing LockRetryPolicy MaxRetries/MaxElapsed budget. The guard is never held across ordinary os.Mkdir, metadata publication, callbacks, retry sleep, git/harness operations, or private tombstone removal, and only the successful renamer deletes its exact tombstone.
10. Unix and Windows guard implementations use one stable sidecar inode and keyed same-process coordination. Because the current agent dependency graph does not cross-compile for Windows, a self-contained file-list command compiles the guard's Windows implementation with GOOS=windows GOARCH=amd64 using go test -c; the bead must name the final guard source/test files in this command after implementation.
11. cd cli && go test -race ./internal/agent/... -run "TestTrackerStaleLockBreak(SingleWinner|LoserNeverDeletesWinner)" -count=20 passes.
12. cd cli && go test -race ./internal/agent/... -run "Test(TrackerLockLeaseReleaseDoesNotDeleteReplacement|TrackerStaleLockGuardCrashReleasesWithoutReplacingSidecar|TrackerStaleLockGuardContentionRespectsPolicyDeadline|TrackerLockRuntimePathsIgnoredWithoutGitignore)" -count=20 passes.
13. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s passes.
14. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Two contenders can both observe a stale process-shared main-git lock and mutate the canonical lock pathname from different snapshots. A unique tombstone name alone does not make stale breaking single-winner. A stable stale-break guard alone is also insufficient when the age rule supersedes a still-live holder: that old holder can later release by pathname and delete the replacement. A permanent guard sidecar also becomes DDx runtime dirt unless every git/checkpoint/checkout-sync surface excludes it independently of project .gitignore state.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:215-278 retries os.Mkdir on the canonical lock and classifies directories or malformed regular files before mutating the pathname. cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:309-328 reads pid/acquired_at and removes the canonical directory without serializing observation and mutation across processes. Lines 323-328 may classify an over-age lock stale even when its PID is still live; cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_windows.go:7-16 makes age the only effective stale criterion on Windows. cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:282 later releases every holder with unconditional RemoveAll.

Counterexample 1: contenders A and B inspect the old stale directory; A disposes it and installs a fresh canonical lock; delayed B renames/removes that pathname and deletes A's lock. Unique tombstone names do not protect the source identity.

Counterexample 2: live holder A becomes age-stale, breaker B safely disposes A and acquires a replacement, then delayed A releases by pathname and deletes B.

The malformed stale regular-file branch at cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:263-270 has the same classify-then-pathname-remove race. Acquisition at lines 224-230 also ignores metadata publication errors and currently has no owner identity suitable for safe release.

A stable sidecar must additionally be excluded from synthesized git-add pathspecs at cli/internal/agent/gitops_real.go:165-205, pre-dispatch dirt at cli/internal/agent/predispatch_dirty.go:477-488, and checkout-sync deferral at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:923-931.

Phase 0 WB-5 requires a single-winner cross-process stale-break protocol at docs/helix/06-iterate/phase0-stop-the-self-harm-plan-2026-07-13.md:124-132.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a package-local crash-safe cross-process transition guard for the process-shared main-git lock. Use a never-deleted sibling advisory-lock file with a deterministic name ending in .lock, plus a keyed same-process lock. The OS lock must release automatically on handle close or process death. Ordinary acquisition and release must never delete or recreate the sidecar inode/path. Non-contention open/lock errors fail safe.

Internally change acquisition to return a lease containing the canonical path and a strong unique owner token while preserving the existing withMainGitLock, WithMainGitLock, and withTrackerLock callback APIs. On successful os.Mkdir, write pid, acquired_at, and owner_token successfully before publishing acquisition. withTrackerLockPolicy defers lease Release and joins any release error with the callback error.

Stale break acquires the transition guard within the existing LockRetryPolicy budget, re-reads and reclassifies the current canonical path, and renames only the freshly inspected stale object to a contender-unique sibling tombstone ending in .lock. Remove only the exact tombstone produced by a successful rename. Apply the same guarded reclassification to malformed stale regular files. Release the guard before retrying ordinary os.Mkdir. Preserve existing OR semantics: valid dead PID or valid over-age acquired_at is sufficient; missing/malformed metadata alone is not.

Lease release acquires the same transition guard within a bounded policy, re-reads canonical owner_token, and removes the canonical directory only when the token exactly matches the lease. Missing or mismatched tokens mean the lease was superseded and release preserves the replacement. If guarded release cannot be proven, return an error and leave the lock in place; never fall back to unconditional RemoveAll.

Keep the guard outside callbacks, retry sleeps, ordinary git operations, metadata publication, and private tombstone removal. Add explicit synthesize-commit, pre-dispatch-dirt, and checkout-sync exclusions for the stable sidecar that work even when project .gitignore is absent or stale. Use one package-local path constant/helper so these surfaces cannot drift.

Add deterministic subprocess/barrier tests with signals emitted after actual advisory-guard acquisition and at internal transition stages, not sleep-based ordering, for delayed stale observers, live-age-stale delayed release, deadline behavior, crash-released guard ownership, stable sidecar identity, byte-identical replacement metadata, and git/runtime-path exclusions.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change tracker mutation semantics, tracker record format, trackerLockStaleAge, LockRetryPolicy defaults, lockmetrics caps, harness/Fizeau behavior, bead-store locks, git index locks, or the separate execution-cleanup lock in cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_loop.go:116-156. Do not extract a shared lock package; ddx-dffb7454 and ddx-3f156500 own separate sites and Phase 1 WB-8 owns unification.

PARENT
ddx-8b22914e

DEPS
None. ddx-dffb7454 is a related collection-lock implementation, not a prerequisite or shared-package dependency.

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go renamed to drain_loop.go (or work_loop.go if preferred).
2. grep -rn 'execute_bead_loop' cli/ returns 0 hits.
3. All import paths and references updated to new filename.
4. cd cli && go build ./... green.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go was named when the command was 'ddx agent work'. The command is now 'ddx work' (with ddx agent work as a back-compat alias), and the FEAT-010 refactor (ddx-5cb6e6cd) moves to a work package. The filename no longer matches the command surface or the package intent.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: the file contains the Drain loop logic. Its name derives from the pre-refactor 'execute-bead-loop' command, which has been superseded by 'ddx work'.
- C13 (ddx-387a0178) plans 'file/identifier rename per refactor proposal §3'. If C13 only renames identifiers (not the file), this bead handles the file rename. If C13 subsumes the file rename, this bead should be closed as duplicate — verify after C13 lands.

PROPOSED FIX
- After C13 (ddx-387a0178) lands: confirm whether execute_bead_loop.go was renamed or only its identifiers.
- If file was NOT renamed: rename cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go → drain_loop.go (preferred: matches 'drain the bead queue' conceptual model) or work_loop.go.
- Update all import paths and references.
- Verify: grep -rn 'execute_bead_loop' cli/ returns 0 hits (file and all references gone).

NON-SCOPE
- Identifier renames (those belong to C13).
- Moving the file to a different package.
notes: Superseded by ddx-f088a3fd / ddx-b194d20f and audited by ddx-bdc0065e. This rename assumes the retained ddx agent / cli/internal/agent execution tree, so it must not dispatch after the remove-not-deprecate decision.
title: rename: execute_bead_loop.go → drain_loop.go (or work_loop.go) — file name no longer matches command

acceptance:
1. TestExecutionCleanup_ReclaimsMetadataMarkedHostGlobalScratch proves an old host-global candidate with cleanup.json for the current project still passes liveness checks and is reclaimed.
2. TestExecutionCleanup_ReclaimsMetadataLessPrivateScratch proves a metadata-less candidate inside an explicitly configured project-private scratch boundary remains eligible for safe legacy reclamation.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "TestExecutionCleanup_ReclaimsMetadataMarkedHostGlobalScratch|TestExecutionCleanup_ReclaimsMetadataLessPrivateScratch" -count=1 -timeout 60s passes.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The cleanup flow must still reclaim stale scratch directories when cleanup metadata proves ownership, even if the root is host-global.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:667-681 currently conflates missing metadata with owned scratch. cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:738-755 performs the actual tree accounting and deletion after that decision, so the ownership gate must be precise.

PROPOSED FIX
Keep the existing liveness and project checks for metadata-marked candidates. If cleanup.json proves the directory belongs to the current project and the candidate is stale, reclaim it normally even when it lives under a host-global or legacy scratch root.

NON-SCOPE
Do not add cleanup time budgets; ddx-de1ff4b4 owns bounded reclamation. Do not migrate scratch producers; ddx-6a2ce3bd owns producer placement. Do not disable cleanup of metadata-marked stale paths or weaken active-worktree preservation.

PARENT
None; root cleanup ownership regression.

DEPS
None.
title: retain reclamation for metadata-marked scratch roots

acceptance:
1. TestOwnedScratchMarkerRoundTrip proves the shared marker records resource kind, owner PID, creation time, and available process-start identity and is written atomically.
2. TestFixtureBinaryScratchWritesLiveOwnerMarker exercises both BuildDDxBinary and BuildDDxFizeauTestSeamBinary creation paths and proves each writes a valid live-owner marker before its binary path is cached or returned.
3. TestExecutionCleanup_PreservesLiveFixtureBinaryScratch creates old ddx-fixture-bin-* and ddx-fizeau-testseam-bin-* directories owned by the current live process and proves cleanup preserves both with a live-owner observation even after the ordinary minimum age.
4. TestExecutionCleanup_ReclaimsDeadFixtureBinaryScratch creates old directories for both prefixes with conclusively dead owner identities and proves cleanup removes both and reports their reclaimed bytes and inodes.
5. TestExecutionCleanup_PreservesUnownedFixtureBinaryScratch proves missing, malformed, or unverifiable owner markers under a host-global root are preserved with an unowned or uncertain-owner observation and are never measured or removed solely by prefix and age.
6. TestExecutionCleanup_DefaultPrefixesRequireFixtureOwnerMarker proves ddx-fizeau-testseam-bin-* enters defaultExecutionCleanupScratchPrefixes only with the owner-marker preservation path wired and both fixture prefixes use the same ownership and liveness policy.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/scratchowner/... ./internal/testutils/... ./internal/agent -run "Test(OwnedScratchMarkerRoundTrip|FixtureBinaryScratchWritesLiveOwnerMarker|ExecutionCleanup_(PreservesLiveFixtureBinaryScratch|ReclaimsDeadFixtureBinaryScratch|PreservesUnownedFixtureBinaryScratch|DefaultPrefixesRequireFixtureOwnerMarker))$" -count=1 passes.
8. cd cli && go test ./internal/scratchowner/... ./internal/testutils/... ./internal/agent/... passes.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
BuildDDxBinary and BuildDDxFizeauTestSeamBinary create process-lifetime binary directories shared through sync.Once. After ddx-b2702b9f moves both directories beneath configured execution scratch, cleanup must eventually reclaim abandoned directories without deleting one still owned by a long-running test process. The cleanup allowlist currently recognizes ddx-fixture-bin-* but not ddx-fizeau-testseam-bin-*; adding the missing prefix without ownership evidence would make directory age alone sufficient to delete a live process-owned binary directory.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/testutils/fixture_repo.go:87-95 stores both built binary paths in process-wide sync.Once state, and cli/internal/testutils/fixture_repo.go:119-167 creates and publishes the two binary directories without a durable owner marker.
cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:26-46 includes ddx-fixture-bin-* in defaultExecutionCleanupScratchPrefixes but omits ddx-fizeau-testseam-bin-*.
cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:667-681 synthesizes ownership when cleanup.json is absent, while cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:728-755 removes an allowlisted directory after the age threshold when no liveness evidence preserves it.
cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:189-221 preserves a directory with live PID or lease evidence only when compatible metadata exists.
ddx-05e3d16b and ddx-1eb1ba93 establish the host-global trust and metadata-marked reclamation rules, but neither makes the fixture-binary producer emit process-lifetime ownership evidence.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a small lower-level scratch-owner marker contract that can be imported by both internal/testutils and internal/agent without an import cycle. The marker must record the resource kind, owner PID, creation time, and a process-start identity when the platform exposes one; marker writes must be atomic.
Have BuildDDxBinary and BuildDDxFizeauTestSeamBinary write the marker immediately after creating their directory and before caching or returning the binary path. If marker creation fails, remove the new directory and fail the build helper instead of publishing an unowned path.
Teach execution cleanup to recognize both ddx-fixture-bin-* and ddx-fizeau-testseam-bin-* through the owner-marker contract. Preserve an old directory while its recorded owner process identity is live. Reclaim it only after the owner is dead or conclusively mismatched and the existing minimum-age requirement is satisfied.
When process identity cannot be validated safely, preserve the directory and emit a structured uncertain-owner observation. Missing or malformed markers must follow the ownership rules from ddx-05e3d16b and ddx-1eb1ba93; a prefix alone is never host-global ownership proof.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/testutils/fixture_repo.go and fixture_repo_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go and execution_cleanup_test.go
- a small shared internal scratch-owner marker package and its tests

NON-SCOPE
Do not change Fizeau routing, test-seam behavior, harness selection, model selection, or provider policy.
Do not replace the process-wide sync.Once binary cache with t.TempDir.
Do not redesign general host-global scratch trust; ddx-05e3d16b and ddx-1eb1ba93 own that policy.
Do not reclaim a metadata-less or uncertain-owner host-global directory merely because its name matches a DDx prefix.
Do not change execution scratch placement; ddx-b2702b9f owns that migration.

PARENT
ddx-f914dddd

DEPS
ddx-b2702b9f must land first so both producers use the configured scratch root.
ddx-1eb1ba93 must land first so metadata-marked stale reclamation and host-global ownership rules are established; it already depends on ddx-05e3d16b.
title: cleanup: require live-owner evidence for fixture-binary scratch

acceptance:
1. TestRunnerEmbeddedDispatchHomesDoNotUsePrivateGoModuleCache exercises the LM Studio and OpenRouter embedded-dispatch fixtures and proves their isolated HOME never gains go/pkg/mod.
2. TestRunnerEmbeddedDispatchHomesDoNotCreateGlobalDDXHome records matching entries beneath os.TempDir before and after the fixture and proves no new ddx-home-* directory is created.
3. TestRunLMStudioDispatchesThroughEmbeddedAgent and TestRunOpenRouterDispatchesThroughEmbeddedAgent retain their no-real-provider and no-exec-path assertions while using a cleanup-checking private HOME helper.
4. The fixture gives child Go work an explicit module-cache/GOPATH policy captured before HOME isolation; cleanup errors are no longer discarded.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "Test(RunnerEmbeddedDispatchHomesDo(NotUsePrivateGoModuleCache|NotCreateGlobalDDXHome)|Run(LMStudio|OpenRouter)DispatchesThroughEmbeddedAgent)$" -count=1 -timeout 30s passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The two embedded-dispatch runner tests isolate provider configuration by changing HOME to a raw ddx-home-* directory. On Niflheim, an abandoned ddx-home-3567057526 contained a 468 MB private Go module cache with read-only module directories. A later internal/agent gate discovered that prefix, recursively walked it, and produced more than 2.7 GB of Go test operation logs before continuing. Repeated gates can duplicate the module cache, leak inodes, and make cleanup expensive.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go:29-34 and cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go:56-61 call os.MkdirTemp with an empty root, change HOME, and discard os.RemoveAll errors. Changing HOME also changes the default GOPATH and GOMODCACHE for any Go subprocess or embedded tool that does not receive explicit values. Downloaded module directories are commonly mode 0555, so the ignored cleanup error can leave the entire test HOME under the process-global temp root. These are the only ddx-home-* producers in cli/internal/agent.

PROPOSED FIX
Replace the raw os.MkdirTemp fixtures with a shared test helper backed by t.TempDir so cleanup failures are test failures. Before changing HOME, preserve an explicit stable Go module-cache/GOPATH policy for child work so the isolated provider-config HOME cannot become a second module cache. Add a tripwire that runs both embedded-dispatch paths and proves no HOME/go/pkg/mod tree and no new process-global ddx-home-* directory is created.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go
- nearest internal/agent test helper if shared setup is warranted

NON-SCOPE
Do not change production runner routing, make live provider calls, delete existing host scratch, or change production cleanup ownership/budgets. ddx-f914dddd and ddx-de1ff4b4 own those cleanup changes.

PARENT
ddx-6a2ce3bd.

DEPS
None; this test-producer repair can land independently of the production scratch-root helper children.

acceptance:
1. `TestFederationForwardedWriteIdempotency_WorkerStart` or an equivalent e2e assertion proves retrying the same hub-to-spoke worker start request id returns the original worker and does not create a second spoke-local worker.
2. `TestFederationForwardedWriteIdempotency_BeadOrDocumentWrite` or an equivalent e2e assertion proves retrying the same hub-to-spoke bead or document write request id does not append duplicate bead events or duplicate file writes.
3. The tests assert the dedupe key/request id is present in the forwarded envelope or audit metadata.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/federation/... ./internal/server/... ./internal/server/graphql/... -run 'Idempotency|ForwardedWrite|Federation' -count=1` passes.\n5. If Playwright is used, `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bunx playwright test e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts` or the new sibling e2e file passes.\n6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Forwarded federation writes need an autonomous idempotency proof. Retrying the same hub-issued command with the same request id must replay the original result and must not launch duplicate workers, append duplicate bead events, or write the same document twice.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:338 defines TC-017.5 idempotency for federated worker commands.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:379 defines TC-020.6 idempotency for mutating managed-node commands.
- docs/helix/06-iterate/server-operator-workbench-plan-2026-06-08.md:98-102 requires forwarded commands to carry request id, target node/project, and expected version metadata.
- Existing GraphQL idempotency tests such as cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_runs_test.go:101-154 and resolver_operator_prompt_test.go:218-231 cover local idempotency surfaces, but the federation owner-write path needs its own forwarded-command coverage.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add focused Go tests or live multi-process tests for forwarded federation write idempotency.
- Use the same request id/idempotency key for two identical hub-issued worker, bead, or document write commands.
- Assert the second response returns the original result/deduplicated state and does not duplicate spoke-local state.
- Cover at least two command classes: worker start and one persistent data mutation (`beadCreate` or `documentWrite`).
- Keep the test deterministic with loopback servers and synthetic request ids; do not rely on wall-clock sleeps beyond short polling for process readiness.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not build a new global idempotency framework unless existing request-id plumbing cannot support the tests.
- Do not add real network/tailnet tests.
- Do not change unrelated local run/operator-prompt idempotency behavior.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-a038a090. Depends on live owner-targeted write coverage so the idempotency assertions attach to a proven forwarding path.
title: federation: prove forwarded write idempotency

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel e
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Sentinel errors declared as distinct `error` values. Test: `TestSentinelErrors_AreErrorValues`.
- Context typed accessors implemented. Tests: `TestWithIdentity_RoundTrip`, `TestIdentityFromContext_AbsentReturnsFalse`, `TestWithTrace_RoundTrip`.
- Foundational sub-interfaces (`BeadInitializer`, `BeadReader`, `BeadLifecycle`, `BeadEventReader`, `BeadEventWriter`) declared with ctx-first signatures. `*Store` satisfies only the non-event foundation interfaces in this bead — compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadInitializer = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadLifecycle = (*Store)(nil)`. No `BeadEventReader`/`BeadEventWriter` assertion is added until E.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

acceptance:
1. Add TestExecuteBeadPromptRequiresSequentialValidationCommands covering claude, codex, opencode, unknown, agent, fiz, and embedded harness selectors; each prompt must say to wait for a focused gate to finish and pass before starting the broader gate and must forbid overlapping validation processes.
2. The prompt explicitly names go test, cargo test, npm test, make test, and lefthook as validation commands that must not overlap, while allowing parallelism internal to one command.
3. TestPrompts_ClaudeVariant_ByteIdentical and TestPrompts_AgentVariant_ByteIdentical pass after updating both checked-in prompt snapshots.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "Test(ExecuteBeadPromptRequiresSequentialValidationCommands|Prompts_ClaudeVariant_ByteIdentical|Prompts_AgentVariant_ByteIdentical)$" -count=1 -timeout 60s passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Execute-bead providers can launch a focused test and the full package suite concurrently. On 2026-07-13, the ddx-12a41acc provider stated that it would run the exact focused regex first and only then the full internal/agent suite, but emitted both tool calls in the same turn. That duplicated Go compilation, started the expensive suite before the focused proof was known green, and increased the chance of CPU and provider-process fan-out.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2104-2112 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2139-2147 require git/index mutations to be sequential but do not impose the same ordering on validation commands. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2085-2094 governs reruns of long commands but does not forbid overlapping focused tests, full suites, linters, or lefthook. Rich harnesses may issue multiple shell tool calls concurrently in one model turn, so narrative intent to run focused-then-full is not an execution constraint.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a shared execute-bead instruction requiring validation gates to run sequentially: wait for focused tests to complete successfully before launching broader package/workspace tests, then wait before lint or pre-commit. Explicitly forbid overlapping go test, cargo test, npm test, make test, and lefthook invocations in the same attempt. Preserve legitimate parallelism inside a single build/test command.

NON-SCOPE
Do not globally disable model tool parallelism, serialize read-only inspection, reduce language toolchain internal parallelism, skip any required gate, or weaken test timeouts. Resource enforcement and host-wide concurrency caps remain owned by ddx-c4cbf253 and ddx-93f6041e.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-5958e8a. Depends on ddx-aa6cc320 so its internal/agent verification cannot launch real provider fixtures.

acceptance:
1. TestExecuteBead_StatusOpenRationaleSkipsSynthesizeCommit uses a runner that writes a valid status: open rationale and leaves a tracked dirty file, then proves SynthesizeCommit and its hook runner are never called and no implementation or iteration commit is created.
2. TestExecuteBead_StatusOpenRationalePreservesDirtyPatch proves the same attempt remains task_no_changes/retryable, records a durable bundle-relative dirty rescue patch, and the patch contains the implementation edit after the isolated worktree is cleaned.
3. TestExecuteBead_MixedImplementationAndNoChangesRationaleStillFails remains green and proves an explicit agent implementation commit plus rationale is still rejected as mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale.
4. TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_DirtyWorktreeSynthesized remains green and proves a dirty successful exit without a rationale still receives the normal synthesized implementation commit.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "TestExecuteBead_(StatusOpenRationaleSkipsSynthesizeCommit|StatusOpenRationalePreservesDirtyPatch|MixedImplementationAndNoChangesRationaleStillFails)|TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_DirtyWorktreeSynthesized" -count=1 -timeout 60s passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
An execute-bead agent that correctly writes status: open in no_changes_rationale.txt while leaving an uncommitted implementation patch is still sent through a synthesized iteration commit and the full staged pre-commit gate. Live attempt 20260713T105612-7589dd4a spent more than a minute in the race hook after a 745.706-second diagnostic failure, even though the agent explicitly requested retry and no land. This turns the fallback contract into another expensive mixed-result attempt and can discard useful dirty work after classification.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1314-1377 checks IsDirty and calls SynthesizeCommit before reading the bundle rationale. Only afterward, cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1415-1448 reads no_changes_rationale.txt and classifies a synthesized implementation commit plus rationale as mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale. Because RealGitOps.SynthesizeCommit invokes git commit, cli/internal/agent/gitops_real.go:148-218 also runs repository hooks before DDx learns that the attempt was explicitly retryable.

PROPOSED FIX
Read and parse the bundle no_changes_rationale.txt before the synthesis branch. When a valid status: open, status: proposed, status: blocked, or verification_command rationale is present and the agent made no explicit commit, do not call SynthesizeCommit. Preserve any dirty implementation diff as a durable rescue patch in the execution bundle, attach its path to the result, then classify the rationale normally. Retain synthesized commits for dirty successful exits without a rationale, and retain fail-closed mixed-result rejection when the agent itself made an implementation commit and also wrote a rationale.

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale rejection, skip required gates for successful implementation commits, change no-changes retry/close policy, or automatically land the preserved dirty patch.

PARENT
None; root execute-bead fallback-contract regression observed during the Niflheim queue drain.

DEPS
None.

acceptance:
1. TestBeadMigrate_ToAxonVerifyRoutesToImporter covers --to-axon --verify routing Verify true into Migrator.MigrateToAxon.
2. TestBeadMigrate_ToAxonVerifyRoutesToImporter asserts the command prints or returns verification success when the migrator succeeds.

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-53df5e2f requires ddx bead migrate --to-axon --verify to route to the Axon importer in verification mode and report verification success through both command output and JSON stats. This should be testable without coupling to importer storage internals.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The CLI reads flags at cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go:44 and routes --to-axon into Migrator.MigrateToAxon at cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go:55. The relevant flag registration is at cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go:202, and the importer stats shape is defined at cli/internal/bead/migrate.go:609. Without a verify-mode routing test, --to-axon --verify can be accepted by the CLI but fail to pass Verify true or fail to expose verification status to operators.

PROPOSED FIX
Update cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go and cmd tests so --to-axon --verify routes Verify true into Migrator.MigrateToAxon and prints or returns verification success when the migrator succeeds. Ensure JSON and human output expose bead, event, attachment, dry-run, verify, and limit values from the migrator stats.

NON-SCOPE
- Schema-versioning migration ladder; deferred until first v1-to-v2 breaking change.
- Real-wire integration test against actual Postgres; separate WIRE bead.
- Continuous sync between JSONL and Postgres.
- Storage importer internals except as needed to satisfy the Migrator interface; sibling children own importer apply/fidelity behavior.
- New backend selection policy outside the existing Migrator/NewStore construction path.
- Do not claim or edit parent bead ddx-53df5e2f tracker data manually; tracker mutations must go through ddx bead commands.
- Help text, invalid flag-combination rejection, dry-run routing, and apply routing are owned by sibling child beads.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT
TD-027.

DEPENDENCY TO PRESERVE WHEN FILING
Parent dependency ID ddx-3d96c6f9.

verification_command: cd cli && go test ./cmd -run 'TestBeadMigrate_ToAxonVerifyRoutesToImporter' && go test ./...

Investigation findings (code-reading only, no test execution possible):

1. The --to-axon --verify routing already exists and appears correct in cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go:55-96:
   - `Verify: verifyAxon` is passed into `bead.MigrateAxonOptions` at line 67.
   - Non-JSON output prints "Verification passed." when verifyAxon is true (line 93-95).
   - JSON output (migrateAxonStatsView, lines 218-223) embeds bead.MigrateAxonStats (BeadsMigrated, EventsMigrated, AttachmentsMigrated) plus DryRun, Verify, and Limit fields.

2. The gap was in test coverage, not production code: TestBeadMigrate_ToAxonVerifyRoutesToImporter already existed in cli/cmd/bead_migrate_test.go (added by a prior sibling bead) and asserted routing (AC1) but did not assert the human-readable "Verification passed." output (AC2) or the JSON stats exposure (AC3).

3. Change made this attempt (uncommitted, present in this worktree): extended TestBeadMigrate_ToAxonVerifyRoutesToImporter in cli/cmd/bead_migrate_test.go to:
   - Assert the non-JSON `--to-axon --verify` output contains "Verification passed."
   - Run a second invocation with `--to-axon --verify --limit 9 --json`, and assert the decoded JSON contains BeadsMigrated, EventsMigrated, AttachmentsMigrated, DryRun (false), Verify (true), and Limit (9) matching the injected captureAxonMigrator stats/opts.

   No production code changes were made in cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go or cli/internal/bead/migrate.go — the existing implementation already satisfies the routing and output-exposure behavior described in the bead; only the test needed strengthening per AC1-3.

4. This change could not be verified with `go build`/`go test`/`go vet` (no Bash access), so it must NOT be treated as committed or verified. If the worktree survives to retry, the diff in cli/cmd/bead_migrate_test.go is present and should be verified with the command above before committing. If the worktree is reset, re-apply the same test extension (this rationale contains the full intended diff description) and verify then commit.

No AC in this bead required changes to cli/internal/bead/migrate.go — MigrateAxonStats and MigrateToAxon already provide everything cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go needs.

acceptance:
1. TestWork_WatchKillingWorkerReapsProviderChildWithin5s passes reliably when legacy DDx skill symlinks exist in the project root.
2. TestProviderLaunchSkipsProjectRuntimePreflight proves `ddx __provider-launch` does not emit project runtime preflight or doctor diagnostics before execing the provider.
3. TestWorkPreflightStillWarnsOnLegacySkillSymlinks proves `ddx work`/`ddx try` still emit the project-local legacy symlink warning.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/...` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM: DDx worker bead ddx-3452fd6d blocked on `cd cli && go test ./...` because `cli/cmd/work_provider_orphan_linux_test.go::TestWork_WatchKillingWorkerReapsProviderChildWithin5s` never observes the stub codex PID. The worker evidence says the built ddx binary exits during startup with legacy skill symlink diagnostics before the provider-launch shim can exec the stub.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: `cli/cmd/work_provider_orphan_linux_test.go:60-140` builds a real ddx binary and exercises the provider-launch shim end-to-end. `cli/cmd/project_runtime_preflight.go:44-79` and `cli/cmd/doctor.go:407` emit/fail on legacy DDx skill symlink diagnostics. The provider-launch path used by the shim must be isolated from project runtime preflight/doctor diagnostics, but the current test evidence shows those diagnostics can abort the built binary before the stub provider starts.

PROPOSED FIX: Make the `__provider-launch`/shim execution path avoid project runtime preflight and legacy skill symlink diagnostics, or scope the diagnostics so provider-launch remains a minimal wrapper that only sets process attributes and execs the resolved provider. Keep the production preflight warning for `ddx work`/`ddx try`.

NON-SCOPE: Do not weaken the legacy skill symlink doctor check for `ddx doctor`; do not remove the provider orphan test; do not bypass `PR_SET_PDEATHSIG` behavior.

acceptance:
1. procExecutionCleanupAttemptProcessScanner.inspect (cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process_linux.go) falls back to scanning /proc/<pid>/fd/* symlink targets for worktree attribution when cwd-based attribution resolves to an empty Worktree.
2. New test TestProcExecutionCleanupAttemptProcessScanner_FdFallbackAttribution in a `_linux_test.go` real-process test file (following the spawn-and-inspect pattern of orphan_harness_reaper_realproc_linux_test.go) spawns a helper process with cwd outside every known root but an open file descriptor pointing under a fake worktree root, and asserts the scanner attributes proc.Worktree to that root.
3. New test TestProcExecutionCleanupAttemptProcessScanner_NoFallbackWhenNoRootedFd (or equivalent) asserts a process with neither a rooted cwd nor a rooted open fd remains excluded from the scan (Scan omits it), unchanged from current behavior.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run TestProcExecutionCleanupAttemptProcessScanner green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
procExecutionCleanupAttemptProcessScanner.inspect (cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process_linux.go:57-74) drops a process entirely -- it is never even offered to classification -- whenever os.Readlink("/proc/<pid>/cwd") fails or returns empty (execution_cleanup_process_linux.go:63,70-72), or when the resolved cwd no longer maps to any known root. Such a process is invisible to the leftover-worktree scan even though it may still hold open files inside a leased worktree (design: docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-040-leftover-worktree-process-reaping.md, "Change 2").

ROOT CAUSE
Worktree attribution today has exactly one signal: cwd (executionCleanupAttemptProcessFromWorkerStatus, execution_cleanup_process.go:415-437, fed from procExecutionCleanupAttemptProcessScanner.inspect at execution_cleanup_process_linux.go:63). If proc.Worktree ends up empty, inspect returns `(executionCleanupAttemptProcess{}, false)` at execution_cleanup_process_linux.go:70-72 and the process is dropped from the scan entirely -- it never reaches classifyExecutionCleanupAttemptProcessGroup, so it can never be reaped no matter how long it has been orphaned.

PROPOSED FIX
In procExecutionCleanupAttemptProcessScanner.inspect, when cwd-based attribution yields an empty Worktree, add a fallback: enumerate /proc/<pid>/fd/*, resolve each entry's symlink target (best-effort, skipping unreadable descriptors), and apply the same root-truncation rule used for cwd attribution (reuse or extract the truncate-to-root helper introduced or existing in execution_cleanup_process.go) against each resolved target. The first fd target that truncates to a root under the configured tempRoot becomes Worktree. This is strictly additive: cwd-based attribution keeps priority, and a process with no cwd signal and no matching fd signal remains unclassified (excluded from the scan, never killed), preserving today's conservative default.

NON-SCOPE
- No change to the classification/kill-safety logic in classifyExecutionCleanupAttemptProcessGroup -- this bead only widens the detection signal feeding it.
- No non-Linux implementation -- mirrors the existing execution_cleanup_process_other.go no-op split.
- No change to the cwd-based root-normalization fix (separate child bead, ddx-79dab565).

acceptance:
1. Add `TestServerShutdownBoundsSupervisorWaitBeforeWorkerShutdown` (or an equivalently named test) in `cli/internal/server/server_shutdown_test.go` that simulates a supervisor goroutine stuck after cancellation and proves `Shutdown()` still invokes worker cleanup within a bounded duration.
2. Add or update a worker-manager shutdown regression so a live managed worker with descendant git/provider subprocesses is reaped through the existing worker shutdown path even when supervisor cancellation is slow.
3. `Server.Shutdown()` must not block indefinitely on `supervisorDone`; timeout behavior must return/log a useful error while continuing to close bead hub and stop land coordinators.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./cmd/...` exits 0.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` exits 0.
description:
PROBLEM: Restarting the DDx user service during active supervised work can hang until systemd times out, leaving systemd to SIGKILL server child processes. Live evidence from 2026-07-07 03:05:19 EDT: `ddx server stop` did not return until systemd marked `ddx-server.service` failed with `Result: timeout` and killed leftover git subprocesses, interrupting the Axon worker attempt that was in post-execution handling.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: `cli/internal/server/server.go:379-385` cancels the supervisor and then waits on `supervisorDone` with no timeout. Worker cleanup is only reached afterward at `cli/internal/server/server.go:388-395`. The worker shutdown path itself has a bounded wait (`cli/internal/server/workers.go:1696-1703`), but it is unreachable while the supervisor goroutine is stuck in a reconcile/git operation. The installed systemd unit generated from `cli/cmd/server_service.go:26-42` has no explicit stop timeout or ExecStop mitigation, so the default systemd timeout eventually kills the whole cgroup.

PROPOSED FIX: Make server shutdown bounded and ordered so worker cleanup always runs promptly even when supervisor reconciliation is slow or stuck. A good implementation is to cancel the supervisor, wait only a short bounded grace for `supervisorDone`, continue to `supervisorRegistry.Shutdown()` / `workers.Shutdown()` on timeout, and surface/log the supervisor timeout without preventing worker cleanup.

NON-SCOPE: Do not rewrite worker execution semantics, change bead claim/lease policy, or alter unrelated service install behavior.

Parent: none. Dependencies: none.

reason=Bash tool sandbox is unusable in this execution worktree — every shell invocation fails immediately with: ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/-Users-erik-Projects--ddx-exec-wt--execute-bead-wt-ddx-24949caf-20260708T152102-77b1e4a1' This is the harness's per-session sandbox directory under /tmp/claude-501, not this repo's disk. It fails even for trivial no-op commands (`true`, `echo ok`), confirming the host's /tmp (or the claude-501 sandbox mount) is out of space/inodes rather than anything the bead's code change could affect. Retried the shell 3 times; ENOSPC persisted every time. Effect: I could not run `go test`, `git status/add/commit`, or `lefthook run pre-commit` — every AC in this bead (ddx-24949caf) requires at least one of those. I read the relevant code (cli/internal/server/server.go Shutdown(), cli/internal/server/workers.go waitForStoppedRecord/Shutdown, cli/cmd/server_service.go) and confirmed the root cause described in the bead (unbounded <-s.supervisorDone wait at server.go:379-386, ahead of the already-bounded worker shutdown wait at workers.go:1696-1703) still matches current HEAD, but did not make code edits because without a shell there is no way to compile, test, or commit them, and an uncommitted/unverified change would violate "do not commit red code" / "commit exactly once when green". This is a host resource-exhaustion problem external to the ddx-24949caf bead scope (server shutdown ordering). It needs operator or orchestrator action to free /tmp/claude-501 space (or reschedule this execution onto a worktree/host with available space) before this bead can be retried. verification_retry_hint: rerun `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./cmd/...` and `lefthook run pre-commit` once shell access is restored, before assuming this rationale is stale.
