acceptance:
1. TestValidateAttemptIntegrity_MissingRequiredGateEvidenceRejected proves a code-changing attempt whose bead requires lefthook is rejected when GateRuns is empty, failed, interrupted, background-only, or no-staged-files.
2. TestValidateAttemptIntegrity_NoVerifyImplementationCommitRejected proves any implementation-agent git commit --no-verify command rejects the attempt even when another gate run appears meaningful.
3. TestExecuteBead_WiresHarnessGateEvidenceIntoIntegrityValidation simulates a provider that stages code, receives a failed pre-commit gate, commits with --no-verify, and proves the attempt is preserved with failure_mode=attempt_integrity rather than merged.
4. TestExecuteBeadPromptForbidsNoVerify asserts every rich and embedded execute-bead prompt explicitly forbids --no-verify, hook disabling, and committing after failed or interrupted required gates; prompt snapshots are updated.
5. TestValidateAttemptIntegrity_AllowsDDXOwnedNoVerifyCommits proves scoped checkpoint, tracker-only, landing, and durable-audit commits remain permitted because they are outside implementation-agent evidence.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "Test(ValidateAttemptIntegrity_(MissingRequiredGateEvidenceRejected|NoVerifyImplementationCommitRejected|AllowsDDXOwnedNoVerifyCommits)|ExecuteBead_(WiresHarnessGateEvidenceIntoIntegrityValidation|PromptForbidsNoVerify))$" -count=1 -timeout 120s passes.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Attempt 20260713T094206-53684645 for ddx-415c8b95 had a staged pre-commit gate terminated after it recursively walked 100 leaked host-global module caches. The provider then ran git commit --no-verify and DDx accepted, merged, closed, and pushed implementation commit 85bb0fae0. A code-changing attempt can therefore bypass its required staged hook and still be classified as successful.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2108-2113 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2143-2148 require tests and a staged lefthook run but do not explicitly forbid --no-verify, hook disabling, or bypassing a failed gate. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1507-1514 invokes ValidateAttemptIntegrity without populating GateRuns. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_validation.go:160-186 checks gate evidence only when len(GateRuns) is nonzero, and TestValidateAttemptIntegrity_SingleCommitNoEvidenceSkips codifies missing evidence as success. The validation promised by closed bead ddx-725b65b4 is therefore not wired into the live execute-bead path.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add an explicit execute-bead instruction that implementation agents must never use git commit --no-verify, disable hooks, or commit after a required gate failed or was interrupted.
- Capture implementation-agent tool commands and foreground gate outputs/exit codes from the harness session into structured attempt-integrity input.
- When a code-changing bead acceptance requires lefthook run pre-commit, require at least one successful meaningful staged run after the final staged mutation and before the implementation commit. Missing, failed, interrupted, background-only, or no-staged-files evidence must reject and preserve the attempt.
- Treat an implementation-agent git commit --no-verify command as an integrity violation even if a prior gate appeared meaningful.
- Keep DDx-owned checkpoint, tracker-only, landing, and durable-audit commits outside this rule; their scoped --no-verify use is orchestrator-owned.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_validation.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_validation_test.go
- execute-bead prompt guardrail/snapshot tests
- nearest harness-session evidence adapter needed to populate GateRuns and implementation commands

NON-SCOPE
Do not rewrite or remove existing execute-bead history, forbid DDx-owned tracker/audit commits, rerun repository hooks for synthesized commits, or weaken required gates. ddx-a3358532 owns synthesized-commit hook behavior.

PARENT
None; root attempt-integrity regression.

DEPS
Related to closed ddx-725b65b4 and open ddx-2295d9b1, but no dependency is required.

acceptance:
1. TestStaleObservationFails passes against an injected fixture revision: a Complete Verification row whose observation revision differs from the injected current revision fails.
2. TestCurrentRevisionExitZeroObservationPasses passes against an injected fixture revision: a Complete Verification row with a structured observation at the current revision and exit code 0 passes the freshness check.
3. TestNonZeroCurrentObservationFails passes: a Complete or Implemented Verification row with the current revision but non-zero exit code fails.
4. TestObservationProseAssertionDoesNotPass passes: prose stating that a command passed does not satisfy the current-revision observation requirement without structured revision and exit-code evidence.
5. cd cli && go test ./tools/lint/spechonesty/... passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
A Complete or Implemented Helix document can cite a verification observation captured at a stale repository revision, so a checked-in assertion that a command once passed is treated as current proof.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
After the spechonesty scaffold/status-parser child lands, cli/tools/lint/spechonesty/analyzer.go:1 is the analyzer entrypoint but has no observation-freshness check. The governing contract is docs/helix/06-iterate/phase2-doc-truth-plan-2026-07-13.md:68, which requires Complete/Implemented mappings to have current-revision, exit-zero observations and rejects checked-in assertions that a command passed as sufficient proof. docs/helix/06-iterate/phase2-doc-truth-plan-2026-07-13.md:33 and docs/helix/06-iterate/phase2-doc-truth-plan-2026-07-13.md:176 repeat that Complete/Implemented documents require passing observations produced for the current repository revision and no waiver path exists for those statuses.

PROPOSED FIX
Add deterministic read-only observation freshness validation to spechonesty. Inject the current repository revision and any fixture clock/test revision source so unit tests are hermetic and do not fetch network state. For each Complete/Implemented Verification mapping row, require an observation keyed to the injected current revision with exit code 0. Fail rows whose observation revision differs from the current revision, whose exit code is non-zero, or whose observation is only prose rather than structured evidence. Keep the scan scope aligned with WB-1: docs/helix/01-frame/features/ plus docs/helix/02-design/{adr,concepts,contracts,solution-designs,technical-designs}/.

NON-SCOPE
Do not auto-restamp documents, infer proof from prose, fetch network state, or mark aspirational metrics complete. Complete-coverage/waiver logic and status parsing live in sibling children. Prose quality and AC numbering style are out of scope. Duplicate document-id and duplicate US-id detection are out of scope for this child.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT
Phase 2 doc truth plan WB-1 step 4 and exit requirement: docs/helix/06-iterate/phase2-doc-truth-plan-2026-07-13.md:68 and docs/helix/06-iterate/phase2-doc-truth-plan-2026-07-13.md:176.

PARENT: ddx-af66f903. SOURCE/PARENT BEAD: ddx-2e992616. DEPS: the spechonesty scaffold/status-parser child that provides cli/tools/lint/spechonesty/ and status/verification-row parsing.
title: Add spechonesty current-revision observation failures

2. TestClosureGateRejectionPropagatesToLoop proves the final loop report is not success, records land_status not_landed or closure rejected, and leaves the bead open.
3. Existing valid CloseWithEvidence and work-loop closure tests remain green.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... -run "Test(CloseWithEvidenceReturnsErrClosureGateRejectedAndLeavesOpen|ClosureGateRejectionPropagatesToLoop)" -count=1 passes.

description:
PROBLEM
A rejected closure evidence gate can leave the bead open while the work loop reports success and continues as if closure completed.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/bead/store.go:2031-2070 rejects invalid evidence, but cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4257-4268 does not project that rejection into the loop outcome. This is Phase 0 WB-7b at docs/helix/06-iterate/phase0-stop-the-self-harm-plan-2026-07-13.md:145-153.

PROPOSED FIX
Return a typed ErrClosureGateRejected from the store and require the work loop to classify it as not closed/not successful, preserve evidence, release the claim under existing policy, and continue only with an explicit retry or operator disposition.

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken closure evidence, auto-close after rejection, change candidate review semantics, or reinterpret repository evidence.

PARENT
ddx-8b22914e.

DEPS
None.

description:
PROBLEM
After an execution provider exits with task_failed and leaves implementation edits, DDx synthesizes an iteration commit. That commit runs the repository pre-commit hook even though the provider already ran or failed the same gate. On 2026-07-13, the failed ddx-0f29e87a attempt entered the internal/agent race suite a second time during synthesis and recreated real Codex provider processes, consuming CPU and preventing prompt failure finalization.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1297-1383 invokes GitOps.SynthesizeCommit whenever the attempt worktree is dirty and HEAD did not move, including when exitCode is non-zero and the preliminary outcome is task_failed. cli/internal/agent/gitops_real.go:151-180 stages the implementation diff and executes plain git commit -m, so repository hooks run inside DDx orchestration. The durable DDx audit paths already use --no-verify at cli/internal/agent/durable_audit.go:125-128 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1724-1731, demonstrating the intended separation between orchestration-owned commits and user-invoked validation.

PROPOSED FIX
Make RealGitOps.SynthesizeCommit create the DDx-owned synthesized attempt commit without invoking repository hooks. Preserve the substantive dirty diff and the per-attempt commit-message/audit contract. Keep validation in the provider run and DDx gate/review pipeline; do not silently reinterpret a failed attempt as success.

NON-SCOPE
Do not skip provider-requested gates, landing gates, or operator git hooks. Do not discard failed implementation edits or rewrite existing execute-bead history. Do not weaken test assertions or timeouts.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-5958e8a. Depends on ddx-aa6cc320 so the package gate cannot itself launch real provider fixtures while this fix is verified.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx pins Fizeau v0.14.50, whose subprocess discovery refresher discards its bounded context and whose Codex model discovery launches ptyquota with context.Background. A cancelled DDx request or stopped server can therefore return while codex --no-alt-screen continues consuming CPU and writing caller-owned temp/cache state. DDx-side containment wrappers reduce symptoms but do not repair the dependency lifecycle.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/go.mod:7 pins github.com/easel/fizeau v0.14.50.
- In that release, Fizeau internal/serviceimpl/subprocess_discovery_cache.go:74-94 ignores the discoverycache.Refresher context before calling DefaultModelSnapshot.
- In that release, Fizeau internal/harnesses/codex/model_discovery.go:20-48 calls ptyquota.Run(context.Background(), ...).
- cli/cmd/lifecycle_model_discovery_test.go proves DDx's outer containment helper, but a local wrapper test cannot prove the Fizeau cache refresher or PTY receives cancellation.

PROPOSED FIX
Wait for upstream fizeau-d25b2862 to add a context-aware model-discovery contract, pass the refresh context through subprocessDiscoveryRefresher, and reap the Codex PTY on cancellation. After an upstream release contains that closing commit, bump cli/go.mod and cli/go.sum to that release. Add DDx integration coverage proving the service lifecycle cancellation reaches dependency discovery, returns only after the probe is reaped, and produces no late cache/home writes. Verify the existing embedded dispatch temp-root regressions with the released dependency.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/go.mod
- cli/go.sum
- cli/cmd/lifecycle_model_discovery_test.go and the nearest DDx service-lifecycle integration fixture

NON-SCOPE
Do not patch Fizeau source in the DDx repository, add a local replace directive, certify the existing outer containment wrapper as the upstream fix, disable discovery, extend the two-second caller timeout, kill manual Codex sessions, or change route selection.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-f84ee9a7. Externally blocked by fizeau#fizeau-d25b2862. Related to ddx-092f0d34 and regression ddx-403e9a23.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx estimates missing cost from concrete model and harness identity, accepts its own local price input, and stores execution cost in a value-only field. That makes absent cost indistinguishable from explicit zero and presents DDx guesses as accounting facts. Cost must be only the value Fizeau reported, with explicit presence preserved from execution through indexing, metrics, CLI, and GraphQL.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/pricing.go:3-35 owns DDx model pricing and EstimateCost. cli/cmd/usage_support.go:54-72 and cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:1274-1323 estimate missing cost from route identity. cli/internal/config/types.go:319-322 still accepts DDx local_per_1k_tokens pricing. cli/internal/agent/types.go:106-133 defines Result.CostUSD as value-only. cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:324-346 copies final cost only when greater than zero. cli/internal/agent/session_index.go:22-40 has CostPresent but lines 206-221 and 240-293 synthesize it from nonzero values. cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:818-820 drops explicit zero.

PROPOSED FIX
Delete cli/internal/agent/pricing.go, EstimateCost, DDx local price configuration, and every model/provider/harness identity cost estimate. Add explicit cost presence at agent.Result and translate the public Fizeau terminal contract exactly: nil is unknown, non-nil 0.0 is known zero, and non-nil positive is known positive. Preserve that distinction through session writes/reindex, usage and process metrics, and GraphQL. Historical records without presence remain unknown. Aggregates sum only present values and expose unknown participation instead of fabricating a complete total.

NON-SCOPE
Do not classify billing mode, add a replacement DDx price table or remote pricing lookup, reinterpret missing as zero/free, change Fizeau pricing, or use reported cost for routing, retry, escalation, review grading, admission, or close policy. Fizeau predicted candidate cost remains audit evidence and is not DDx pricing.

PARENT
ddx-1b3272a6.

DEPS
ddx-00d406ce supplies released Fizeau terminal cost presence and its public conformance proof.
notes: Next executable child after ddx-00d406ce closes. It must consume the one released v0.15 public cost-presence contract and must not repin Fizeau independently. Until then absent versus explicit-zero terminal cost cannot be proven and this bead must remain dependency-waiting.
title: metrics: preserve Fizeau-reported cost presence and delete DDx pricing

description:
PROBLEM
DDx currently guesses that reviewer errors are transport failures from error strings, raises reviewer power/profile on retry, and ultimately fails or parks otherwise valid implementation work. The design requires Fizeau to classify its own terminal transport outcome; DDx may retry one review operation at identical power and passthrough, then downgrade to deterministic-only close with operator-visible evidence.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:7949-7991 ClassifyReviewError uses text and defaults unknown errors to transport.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:869-931 increases MinPower and selects top profiles after prior errors.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:1251-1292 and execute_bead_review_group.go:153-175 return dispatch errors as review failures.
- cli/internal/agent/candidate_cycle.go:315-325 converts any reviewer error to review_malfunction and prevents landing.
- Current pinned Fizeau v0.14.50 in cli/go.mod exposes no typed Cause/Stage field, so this change must wait for ddx-66a8883f and its compatible upstream release/conformance proof.

PROPOSED FIX
Consume the single typed Fizeau terminal review-transport cause from the DDx attempt-policy adapter. Retry exactly once at identical MinPower and byte-identical operator passthrough. If the typed transport failure repeats, emit an operator-visible review-downgraded event and allow deterministic-checks-only close. Do not raise power, select a profile, or fail the implementation because review transport is unavailable. Non-transport review errors remain distinct and follow their typed policy.

NON-SCOPE
Do not classify provider text, implement Fizeau provider retry, add continuation, invoke provider CLIs, or weaken semantic BLOCK/REQUEST_CHANGES findings.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-b8cacbeb. Depends on ddx-642a9f26 plus ddx-66a8883f, the blocked typed Fizeau result consumer. It must not become execution-ready until the compatible upstream contract is pinned and conformance is green.

description:
PROBLEM
The targeted Go pre-commit helper can report success without running any tests when it is invoked from the repository root with the same root-relative cli/... paths that Git and operator diagnostics naturally produce. During execute-bead attempt 20260713T171608-d879ec96, sh scripts/lefthook-go-test.sh cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go returned 0 in 123ms and launched no go process, creating false validation evidence after the real package gate had timed out.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
scripts/lefthook-go-test.sh:8-18 converts each input to a directory, strips a leading cli/, and silently continues when that derived directory does not exist relative to the current working directory. lefthook.yml:11-14 and lefthook.yml:205-216 normally run the helper with cli/ as the command root, but the helper neither establishes nor validates that working-directory contract. From the repository root, cli/internal/agent becomes internal/agent, the directory check fails, and the helper exits with its initial zero status.

PROPOSED FIX
Make the helper resolve the Go module root deterministically from its own repository location or explicitly normalize both repository-root cli/... paths and cli-root internal/... paths before selecting packages. Track every Go path supplied to the helper and fail nonzero with a diagnostic naming any path that cannot be mapped to an existing module package. Preserve targeted package selection, the race detector, the 30-minute per-package timeout, and compatibility with the normal Lefthook cli/ root.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- scripts/lefthook-go-test.sh
- lefthook.yml only if invocation wiring needs an explicit root argument
- a Go or shell-backed regression test under cli/internal/agent that runs the helper with a fake go executable and inspects its arguments

NON-SCOPE
Do not broaden the hook to the full repository, remove -race, raise or remove the timeout, silently ignore unmapped Go paths, or change which staged files Lefthook passes to the command.

PARENT
None; root hook-validation reliability defect discovered during the Niflheim queue drain.

DEPS
None. Related prior hook reliability work is ddx-42a86089; duplicate-hook work is ddx-8a78ee90.

acceptance:
1. TestExecuteBead_ConcurrentWorkers_NoHEADRefRace continues to run five concurrent ExecuteBeadWithConfig calls against one real temporary Git repository and preserves the HEAD compare-and-swap and BaseRev assertions.
2. TestExecuteBead_ConcurrentWorkers_NoHEADRefRace_DoesNotSpawnProviders installs provider-spawn tripwires for codex, claude, gemini, and __provider-launch and proves none are invoked by the fixture.
3. The fixture uses a bounded hermetic AgentRunner implementation; it does not call NewRunner, production Fizeau discovery, network APIs, subscription caches, or real provider binaries.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "^TestExecuteBead_ConcurrentWorkers_NoHEADRefRace(_DoesNotSpawnProviders)?$" -count=1 -timeout 30s passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s passes and leaves no provider descendants whose command line references the test temp root.
6. A failure/timeout path test proves every fixture-owned subprocess is canceled and reaped before the test returns.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Running the required cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... gate launches five real Codex provider processes from TestExecuteBead_ConcurrentWorkers_NoHEADRefRace. In the 2026-07-13 ddx-2502d3c6 attempt, the package test spawned PIDs 225004, 225054, 225173, 225576, and 226111 as __provider-launch ... codex exec; each ran for more than six minutes at about 10-12% CPU. The test package approached its 10-minute timeout, blocking the single queue worker and consuming server-cgroup CPU for a test whose stated concern is only parent-repo Git serialization.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_concurrent_predispatch_test.go:31-55 builds five production NewRunner(Config{}) instances and labels directive-file paths as model names under the obsolete script route. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_concurrent_predispatch_test.go:73-89 then calls ExecuteBeadWithConfig concurrently with those production runners. The current service-backed runner resolves the request to Codex, so each directive path is passed to codex exec as -m instead of being consumed by a hermetic script fixture. The test comments at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_concurrent_predispatch_test.go:35-37 explicitly say agent output is irrelevant. The package previously had to be repaired for the same 600-second budget under ddx-1a0d4d26, but this fixture has regressed into live provider execution.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace the five production runners in this Git-lock regression with an explicit bounded hermetic AgentRunner fixture that returns the minimal no-changes result needed to reach and validate pre-dispatch serialization.
- Preserve the real temporary Git repository, five concurrent ExecuteBeadWithConfig calls, tracker commits, worktree creation, and HEAD CAS assertions.
- Add a provider-spawn tripwire: real/fake codex, claude, gemini, and provider-launch lookup must fail the test immediately if invoked.
- Audit adjacent direct NewRunner plus Harness=script fixtures reached by the normal internal/agent package gate and migrate any that can invoke live providers to the same hermetic boundary.
- Ensure subprocesses are canceled and reaped on test failure or timeout.

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken production provider discovery, reduce the concurrency below five, delete the HEAD race assertions, or skip the test. Live provider integration remains build-tagged/explicit integration coverage, not part of the default package gate.

PARENT
None; root regression bead.

DEPS
None.

title: tests: stop concurrent predispatch fixture from launching real Codex

acceptance:
1. TestSpecHonestyLefthookWiringPresent passes: an assertion (e.g. a wiring test under cli/tools/lint/spechonesty/ that reads lefthook.yml) confirms a spec-honesty stanza exists in the pre-commit block and a corresponding full-tree stanza exists in the ci block, both invoking `go run ./tools/lint/spechonesty`.
2. The wired check is non-mutating: running it against the corpus performs no writes to any document; a fixture-level assertion confirms exit-non-zero on a known-bad fixture and exit-zero on a clean fixture.
3. cd cli && go test ./tools/lint/spechonesty/... passes.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes and includes the non-mutating spec-honesty check.
description:
PROBLEM
The spechonesty linter exists but is never invoked, so false or unverifiable status stamps, duplicate ids, and stale observations are not a CI failure and can land silently.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
lefthook.yml wires evidence-lint (pre-commit block at lefthook.yml:142, full-tree ci gate at lefthook.yml:494) and runtime-lint (lefthook.yml:177, ci gate at lefthook.yml:503) but has no spec-honesty check. The wiring contract is docs/helix/06-iterate/phase2-doc-truth-plan-2026-07-13.md WB-1 (Files/Validation: lefthook.yml and .github/workflows/ci.yml), scoped to features/ plus the five 02-design subdirectories.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a spec-honesty check to lefthook.yml modeled on the existing evidence-lint/runtime-lint stanzas: run `go run ./tools/lint/spechonesty` over the docs/helix corpus (features/ plus the five 02-design subdirectories), scoped to staged doc changes in pre-commit, and add a full-tree gate in the ci block (mirroring the pattern near lefthook.yml:494-536). The check must be strictly non-mutating — it only reports and exits non-zero; it never rewrites documents. Ensure CI produces and validates the current-revision observation report. Do not restamp any document as part of wiring.

NON-SCOPE
Do not auto-restamp documents, infer proof from prose, fetch network state, or mark aspirational metrics complete. The linter's parsing/coverage/duplicate/stale logic lives in the sibling children. Prose quality and AC numbering style are out of scope.

PARENT: ddx-2e992616. DEPS: the spechonesty scaffold child plus the Complete-coverage/waiver child and the duplicate-id/stale-observation child (the check must exercise all implemented rules).

acceptance:
1. TestStopProjectWorkersWaitsForRunWorkerFinalization uses a test-controlled executor finalization barrier and proves worker teardown cannot return after cancellation but before runWorker finalization completes.
2. TestStopProjectWorkersWaitsForRunWorkerFinalization proves teardown returns after the barrier is released, the worker record has a non-zero FinishedAt, and immediate os.RemoveAll of the fixture root succeeds without later worker writes recreating it.
3. stopProjectWorkers delegates to WorkerManager.Shutdown or an equivalent joining helper; it no longer treats runningManagedWorkerCount equal to zero as proof that the goroutine has exited.
4. TestUnpinnedEntryPointsLeaveModelSelectionToFizeau and TestWorkerSupervisor_ActiveBlockedTerminalConsumesOneDesiredSlot retain their existing assertions and pass repeatedly without test temporary-directory cleanup errors.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/server -run "Test(StopProjectWorkersWaitsForRunWorkerFinalization|UnpinnedEntryPointsLeaveModelSelectionToFizeau|WorkerSupervisor_ActiveBlockedTerminalConsumesOneDesiredSlot)$" -count=20 passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The internal/server package intermittently fails after otherwise successful tests because Go cannot remove a test-owned temporary project directory. Observed failures include TestUnpinnedEntryPointsLeaveModelSelectionToFizeau/server-managed leaving files beneath .ddx and TestWorkerSupervisor_ActiveBlockedTerminalConsumesOneDesiredSlot/does_not_suppress_all_starts_when_desired_count_exceeds_one leaving files beneath .ddx/workers. These unrelated tests share the same worker teardown helper.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor_test.go:1252-1271 implements stopProjectWorkers by calling WorkerManager.Stop and then waiting only until runningManagedWorkerCount returns zero.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1684-1697 makes Stop set the in-memory worker state to stopped and return before the runWorker goroutine has completed.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1332-1339 makes List overlay that stopped in-memory state, so runningManagedWorkerCount reaches zero while finalization is still active.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1220-1293 subsequently records FinishedAt and writes the final status.json. That late write races removal of the test-owned project directory.
cli/internal/server/workers_resolve_route_test.go:213-216 and cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor_test.go:545-547 register the faulty helper in the two observed failing fixtures.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1705-1806 already provides WorkerManager.Shutdown, which cancels live workers and waits for in-process runWorker finalization.

PROPOSED FIX
Replace the Stop-plus-state-poll implementation of stopProjectWorkers with a teardown path that delegates to WorkerManager.Shutdown and does not return until runWorker has written its final record.
If a separate error-returning test helper is useful, keep it private to internal/server tests and have stopProjectWorkers delegate to it.
Add TestStopProjectWorkersWaitsForRunWorkerFinalization. Use an injected ExecuteBeadWorker whose executor observes cancellation and then blocks on a test-controlled release channel. Start teardown asynchronously through the error-returning helper, prove teardown has not returned while finalization remains blocked, release the executor, then prove teardown returns, FinishedAt is persisted, and immediate removal of the fixture root succeeds.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the asynchronous contract of WorkerManager.Stop.
Do not change managed subprocess termination, server supervisor cancellation deadlines, worker reconciliation, Fizeau routing, bead execution semantics, or production temporary-directory placement.
Do not weaken or skip either existing failing test.

PARENT
None. This is a standalone package-gate reliability defect.

DEPS
None. WorkerManager.Shutdown already implements the required join.

title: server tests: join in-process workers before fixture teardown
work-last-detail: session closed before Stop hook

acceptance:
1. TestStatusParserRecognizesBodyAndFrontmatter passes.
2. TestCompleteRequiresFullCurrentVerification and TestCompleteRejectsWaiver pass.
3. TestDuplicateDocumentIDFails and TestDuplicateUserStoryIDFails pass.
4. TestStaleObservationFails passes against a deterministic fixture clock/revision.
5. cd cli && go test ./tools/lint/spechonesty/... passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes and includes the non-mutating spec-honesty check.
description:
PROBLEM
A DDx document can claim Complete without proving every acceptance criterion and user story against the current revision, while duplicate IDs and stale observations pass unnoticed.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/tools/lint/spechonesty does not exist and lefthook.yml:149-186 and 501-536 wire other linters but no status/verification coverage gate. Phase 2 WB-1 defines the missing mechanical contract at docs/helix/06-iterate/phase2-doc-truth-plan-2026-07-13.md:58-74.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a deterministic spec-honesty linter that parses body/frontmatter status, requires full current-revision verification maps for Complete, rejects waivers masquerading as proof, detects duplicate document and user-story IDs, and fails stale observations. Wire it into pre-commit without mutating documents.

NON-SCOPE
Do not auto-restamp documents, infer proof from prose, fetch network state, or mark aspirational metrics complete.

PARENT
ddx-2e992616.

DEPS
None for the linter; later status-restamp beads depend on this child.

title: docs lint: enforce current-revision verification coverage
work-last-detail: session closed before Stop hook

acceptance:
1. TestWorkStatusAllProjectsRetainsLiveWorkerListing proves `--all-projects` still lists every live worker and preserves each worker's project_root.
2. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run 'TestWorkStatusAllProjectsRetainsLiveWorkerListing|TestWorkStatusAllProjectsShowsExplicitGlobalView|TestWorkStatusNoLiveWorkersNamesProjectRoot'` passes.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work status --all-projects` must keep reporting live workers even after the missing-desired-worker diagnostic lands elsewhere, but there is no regression coverage proving the global listing stays intact.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/work_status.go:80-116 builds work status only from live worker scans and active work.
cli/cmd/work_status.go:215-221 prints a single all-projects empty-state line, so there is no guard against a future change accidentally hiding live rows or mixing in desired-state diagnostics.

PROPOSED FIX
Add regression coverage for the all-projects live-worker listing and keep the formatter limited to live workers so the missing-desired-worker explanation stays in `ddx worker status`.

NON-SCOPE
Do not add desired-state reporting to `ddx work status`. Do not change the worker-status payload. Do not change restart accounting. Do not weaken byte/inode resource guards. Do not alter the stale operator-attention fixes tracked by ddx-b17cad6d and ddx-4d92f2cf. Do not implement a new classifier.

Governing artifact: ddx-9c53e551.

acceptance:
1. TestFixtureBinaryScratchUsesConfiguredExecutionRoot sets DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR, exercises the fixture-binary scratch helper with both ddx-fixture-bin-* and ddx-fizeau-testseam-bin-* patterns, and proves both directories are created beneath the configured execution scratch root.
2. BuildDDxBinary and BuildDDxFizeauTestSeamBinary both use the helper covered by TestFixtureBinaryScratchUsesConfiguredExecutionRoot while preserving their process-wide sync.Once behavior.
3. TestWorkResourceExhausted_PrintsOperatorMessage, TestWorkResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_StopsBeforeNextClaim, TestTry_FlagsPlumbThrough, TestDoctorProseChecker_MissingVale, TestDoctorProseChecker_WrongValeVersion, TestDoctorProseChecker_VerboseShowsPathAndVersion, and TestDoctor_WarnsOnLegacyModelCatalogFile use test-owned HOME directories and retain their existing assertions.
4. cd cli && ! rg -n "os\\.MkdirTemp\\(\"\", \"ddx-(home|fixture-bin|fizeau-testseam-bin)-" internal/testutils/fixture_repo.go cmd/work_resource_exhausted_test.go cmd/try_test.go cmd/doctor_test.go passes with no matches.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/testutils/... ./cmd/... -run "Test(FixtureBinaryScratchUsesConfiguredExecutionRoot|WorkResourceExhausted_PrintsOperatorMessage|WorkResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_StopsBeforeNextClaim|Try_FlagsPlumbThrough|DoctorProseChecker_(MissingVale|WrongValeVersion|VerboseShowsPathAndVersion)|Doctor_WarnsOnLegacyModelCatalogFile)$" -count=1 passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/testutils/... ./cmd/... passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Shared DDx fixture binaries and several command-test HOME directories still allocate directly beneath the process-global OS temporary directory. These allocations bypass the configured execution scratch root or test-owned cleanup and can concentrate large binaries, configuration trees, and inode use in one host-global location.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/testutils/fixture_repo.go:122 creates ddx-fixture-bin-* with os.MkdirTemp and an empty root.
cli/internal/testutils/fixture_repo.go:150 creates ddx-fizeau-testseam-bin-* the same way.
cli/cmd/work_resource_exhausted_test.go:80 and cli/cmd/work_resource_exhausted_test.go:163 create test-scoped ddx-home-* directories with raw os.MkdirTemp.
cli/cmd/try_test.go:830 creates another test-scoped ddx-home-* directory with raw os.MkdirTemp.
cli/cmd/doctor_test.go:85, cli/cmd/doctor_test.go:129, and cli/cmd/doctor_test.go:179 create additional test-scoped ddx-home-* directories with raw os.MkdirTemp.
cli/internal/config/execution_worktree_root.go:70-78 already provides config.MkdirExecutionScratch, so this child does not need a new production scratch-root abstraction.

PROPOSED FIX
Extract a small fixture-binary scratch-directory helper in cli/internal/testutils/fixture_repo.go and have both BuildDDxBinary and BuildDDxFizeauTestSeamBinary call config.MkdirExecutionScratch with an empty project root and their existing directory patterns. Preserve the sync.Once behavior and process-lifetime sharing; t.TempDir is not appropriate for binaries shared across tests.
Replace the listed command-test HOME allocations with t.TempDir and remove their redundant manual RemoveAll cleanup.
Add cli/internal/testutils/fixture_repo_test.go with a table-driven test covering both fixture-binary patterns under a configured DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR.

NON-SCOPE
Do not modify cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go; ddx-7326dd54 and ddx-e03399c8 own those HOME fixtures and regression tripwires.
Do not modify provider shim creation; ddx-3dbea48e owns that production path.
Do not change Fizeau routing, harness selection, model selection, provider policy, worktree placement, or execution-root fallback semantics.
Do not delete existing host scratch directories.

PARENT
ddx-6a2ce3bd

DEPS
None. config.MkdirExecutionScratch is already present in the current codebase.
title: tests: move remaining DDx scratch fixtures off raw process temp

acceptance:
1. TestCoordinationContract_HTTP runs the same behavioral contract as TestCoordinationContract_Local against a real httptest DDx server, real bead store, and real git repository.
2. TestCoordinationMutation_UnknownResponseRetryIsAlreadyApplied proves retrying one idempotency key does not duplicate tracker events or landing commits.
3. TestCoordinationReconcile_OrderedResumeAndConflict proves contiguous acknowledgement, resumable batches, and structured conflict evidence.
4. The production project-scoped handlers call WorkerManager.LandCoordinators and the existing bead store rather than maintaining parallel claim/landing state.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/agent/... -run "Test(CoordinationContract_HTTP|CoordinationMutation|CoordinationReconcile)" passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Spec-first development · Executable specifications · Audit trail required · Work is a DAG · Drift is debt · Inspect and adapt</principles>
<concerns>go-std | security-owasp | testing</concerns>
<practices>Project-scoped localhost APIs · requireTrusted boundary · real server/git/bead-store integration tests · go test + lefthook</practices>
<adrs>ADR-022 rev 6: the server serializes typed idempotent coordination mutations against bead/git truth and reconciles ordered offline journals.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-002 makes the host daemon the preferred coordination point for manual and managed workers without replacing durable project state.</governing>
</context-digest>

PROBLEM
The server exposes individual bead claim/unclaim handlers and an in-process land coordinator, but workers cannot submit the complete typed coordination protocol or reconcile offline outcomes. There is no server endpoint for landing or journal reconciliation. This child was originally created as ddx-fee5383c on 2026-07-11, but a later whole-tracker rewrite dropped its row while leaving ddx-2e49980d dependent on the missing ID.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/server.go:1096-1106 and 2075-2114 expose standalone claim/unclaim mutations only. cli/internal/server/land_coordinator.go:91-151 serializes landing only for in-process callers. No project-scoped coordination mutation or reconcile handler calls both through a shared contract. Tracker mutation commit c53be76d6 is durable evidence of the dropped predecessor row; the implementation scope below restores the missing roadmap child under a CLI-generated ID.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add project-scoped POST coordination/mutations and coordination/reconcile handlers using the shared types from ddx-3ab6762d.
- Route claim/lease and tracker transitions to the existing bead store and land requests to the per-project LandCoordinator.
- Persist bounded idempotency outcomes so an unknown-response retry returns already_applied rather than duplicating tracker events or commits.
- Return applied, already_applied, or conflict with durable version/revision evidence.

NON-SCOPE
Do not add a server-side picker, parallel claim table, cross-host federation lease, or command wiring. Do not resurrect or manually edit the dropped ddx-fee5383c tracker row.

PARENT
ddx-635ae251.

DEPS
ddx-3ab6762d supplies the shared request/outcome contract and behavioral suite.
title: server: expose idempotent worker coordination and reconcile endpoints

acceptance: 1. Add `TestServerShutdownBoundsSupervisorWaitBeforeWorkerShutdown` (or an equivalently named test) in `cli/internal/server/server_shutdown_test.go` that simulates a supervisor goroutine stuck after cancellation and proves `Shutdown()` still invokes worker cleanup within a bounded duration.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-24949caf is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM: Restarting the DDx user service during active supervised work can hang until systemd times out, leaving systemd to SIGKILL server child processes. Live evidence from 2026-07-07 03:05:19 EDT: `ddx server stop` did not return until systemd marked `ddx-server.service` failed with `Result: timeout` and killed leftover git subprocesses, interrupting the Axon worker attempt that was in post-execution handling.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: `cli/internal/server/server.go:379-385` cancels the supervisor and then waits on `supervisorDone` with no timeout. Worker cleanup is only reached afterward at `cli/internal/server/server.go:388-395`. The worker shutdown path itself has a bounded wait (`cli/internal/server/workers.go:1696-1703`), but it is unreachable while the supervisor goroutine is stuck in a reconcile/git operation. The installed systemd unit generated from `cli/cmd/server_service.go:26-42` has no explicit stop timeout or ExecStop mitigation, so the default systemd timeout eventually kills the whole cgroup.

PROPOSED FIX: Make server shutdown bounded and ordered so worker cleanup always runs promptly even when supervisor reconciliation is slow or stuck. A good implementation is to cancel the supervisor, wait only a short bounded grace for `supervisorDone`, continue to `supervisorRegistry.Shutdown()` / `workers.Shutdown()` on timeout, and surface/log the supervisor timeout without preventing worker cleanup.

NON-SCOPE: Do not rewrite worker execution semantics, change bead claim/lease policy, or alter unrelated service install behavior.

Parent: none. Dependencies: none.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Add `TestServerShutdownBoundsSupervisorWaitBeforeWorkerShutdown` (or an equivalently named test) in `cli/internal/server/server_shutdown_test.go` that simulates a supervisor goroutine stuck after cancellation and proves `Shutdown()` still invokes worker cleanup within a bounded duration.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

acceptance:
1. TestStopStaleDiskEntry_PreservesLivePIDWithFreshClaim starts a real controllable subprocess, writes a running disk record with that PID and a fresh claim lease, invokes stopStaleDiskEntry, and proves the subprocess remains alive, the claim remains in_progress, and the record is not marked stopped.
2. TestStopStaleDiskEntry_ReapsLivePIDWithReclaimableStaleClaim proves the same path terminates the process tree, releases the stale claim, emits bead.stopped, and marks the record stopped when the lease is safely reclaimable.
3. TestSupervisorReconcile_LiveDiskOnlyAttemptSurvivesTicks removes or omits the manager handle for a real live managed-attempt fixture, runs at least three ReconcileAt ticks, and proves the process and fresh claim survive without a replacement duplicate.
4. Preserving a live disk-only worker emits a structured supervisor/lifecycle diagnostic containing worker ID, PID, bead ID, and preservation reason.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run "Test(StopStaleDiskEntry_(PreservesLivePIDWithFreshClaim|ReapsLivePIDWithReclaimableStaleClaim)|SupervisorReconcile_LiveDiskOnlyAttemptSurvivesTicks)" passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
A server-managed ddx work process can be terminated mid-attempt even while its bead lease and worker heartbeat are fresh. On 2026-07-13, worker worker-20260711T204017-68e9 (PID 303304) emitted a fresh heartbeat for ddx-69591a68 at 05:47:15Z, then received cancellation, exited 130 at 05:47:45Z, and was replaced by the supervisor. The attempt had active provider/test descendants and produced no terminal result. This regresses the incident addressed by ddx-57faa17c: that fix preserved the bead claim but did not protect the live process.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1447-1449 calls cleanupManagedWorkerProcessTree for every live PID named by a disk record before cli/internal/server/workers.go:1451-1467 asks staleDiskEntryCanReleaseClaim whether the fresh lease is safe to reclaim. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1586-1614 correctly refuses claim release for fresh or live same-machine leases, but that decision arrives after the process has already been killed and the record is still rewritten stopped at cli/internal/server/workers.go:1470-1482. cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor.go:270-279 sends every snapshot stale ID through this path. TestSupervisorReconcile_FreshClaimingWorkerSurvivesTicks at cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor_test.go:882-962 keeps the live worker in the manager handle map and gives the stale fixture PID 0, so it cannot detect a live PID represented only by a stale disk entry.

PROPOSED FIX
- Classify whether a stale disk record is reclaimable before sending any signal or rewriting its state.
- Preserve or adopt a live same-machine process when its bead lease/heartbeat is fresh, even if the current server lacks an in-memory handle after restart, re-exec, or reconciliation.
- Reap the process tree, unclaim, and mark stopped only when ownership and liveness evidence prove the record is stale.
- Emit a diagnostic lifecycle/supervisor event for preserved live disk records so operators can distinguish adoption/preservation from cleanup.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change desired worker count, generic process cleanup, provider parent-death behavior, or manual/server coordination owned by ddx-635ae251. Do not weaken cleanup of dead PIDs or genuinely stale leases.

PARENT
None; root regression bead.

DEPS
None.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx still duplicates route resolution, provider-error interpretation, retry transport policy, cleanup ownership, and review routing that belong below the DDx/Fizeau boundary.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The governing plan identifies WB-1 through WB-8 at docs/helix/06-iterate/phase1-lower-the-altitude-plan-2026-07-13.md:89-253. DDx route preflight remains wired before Fizeau, provider and model identity still influence control flow, and review/repair can override concrete routing fields instead of expressing route-neutral strength.

PROPOSED FIX
Delete DDx route preflight first, then consume typed Fizeau results through one DDx attempt-policy adapter. DDx may request minimum strength, escalate after failed attempts, and require stronger independent review; it must not select a harness, provider, model, or route. Complete the remaining worktree, liveness, decomposition, closure, and lock-stack work in ordered child beads.

NON-SCOPE
Do not teach DDx Fizeau routing tables. Do not ask DDx to inspect or kill provider descendants. Do not make temporary provider identity a durable work-item field.

PARENT
None; root recovery epic.

DEPS
Phase 0 mechanical exit, except WB-2a route-preflight deletion which is explicitly dependency-free.
title: Epic: lower DDx orchestration to policy over Fizeau

2. Existing `TestExecutor_ProviderSpawnSetsPdeathsigAndSetpgid` and cmd-package `TestProviderLaunchExecsProviderInDedicatedProcessGroup` remain green against an explicit DDx CLI/fake binary, proving production parent-death and process-group behavior is preserved.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-b291b0f6 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
The mandatory `cd cli && go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./internal/agent` gate recursively launches the package test binary as DDx's hidden provider wrapper. During attempt `20260713T190502-df5adc46`, the parent `agent.test` spawned five concurrent `agent.test __provider-launch <real-provider>` helpers for more than three minutes, grew a 666 MB testlog, and left a real `codex --no-alt-screen` process reparented to the managed worker for 12 minutes at about 188 MiB RSS. This makes the package gate itself create the CPU/memory/orphan condition it is supposed to prevent.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:102-113` calls `os.Executable()` unconditionally and passes that path to `EnsureProviderShimOnPATH`.
- `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:152-163` repeats the same unconditional installation for the resolved-service path.
- `cli/internal/agent/provider_spawn.go:83-118` writes each shim as `<ddxBinary> __provider-launch <realBinary>`. Under `go test`, `os.Executable()` is `agent.test`, not the DDx CLI and it has no Cobra `__provider-launch` dispatch. Re-executing it starts the test suite again; the recursive process can install shims and launch real providers that escape the original gate process group.
- `cli/cmd/provider_launch.go:25-68` is the actual hidden-command implementation, and exists only in the DDx CLI command graph.

PROPOSED FIX
Introduce one injectable resolver for the provider-wrapper executable and use it from both service call sites. In package-test builds, install a suite-wide test resolver/guard that refuses to use the current `agent.test` executable as a provider wrapper while leaving direct `EnsureProviderShimOnPATH` tests free to supply an explicit real/fake DDx CLI binary. Fail closed with actionable evidence if a test fixture attempts to install a provider shim from a Go test binary. Preserve production PATH shim, `Pdeathsig`, process-group, and real CLI hidden-command behavior.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not remove provider parent-death protection in production.
- Do not replace Fizeau routing or provider discovery.
- Do not weaken the full `internal/agent` race gate or merely increase its timeout.
- Do not kill unrelated provider processes from other repositories.
- Scanner injection remains owned by `ddx-02200b1e`; fixture-specific spawn tripwires remain owned by `ddx-26bf19c7`.

PARENT AND DEPS
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `TestAgentPackageSuite_DoesNotExecTestBinaryAsProviderLauncher` exercises the suite guard and proves no descendant command line matches `agent.test __provider-launch` and no real codex, claude, gemini, opencode, or pi process is launched.
- Existing `TestExecutor_ProviderSpawnSetsPdeathsigAndSetpgid` and cmd-package `TestProviderLaunchExecsProviderInDedicatedProcessGroup` remain green against an explicit DDx CLI/fake binary, proving production parent-death and process-group behavior is preserved.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
notes: Cancelled: deterministic lifecycle fallback produced a child with no mandatory Go or Lefthook gate. Its suite-guard and production-preservation scope was consolidated atomically into ddx-5653688f.

description:
PROBLEM
While the managed DDx worker decomposed ddx-29491a0e on 2026-07-13, the preclaim-decompose Codex provider remained live for more than two minutes, but `ddx work status --json` changed to active_work.count=0 and reported no bead_id, phase, or last_activity_at. Operators therefore see an apparently idle worker while queue-critical model work is running.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1604-1617 constructs the sidecar liveness reporter but explicitly defers emission until a bead is claimed. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3108-3120 invokes PostAttemptDecompositionHook before claim without setting a resolving phase or ticking liveness.

PROPOSED FIX
Publish candidate-scoped resolving liveness before dispatching preclaim decomposition, heartbeat it while the provider runs, and include provider-child observations without creating a bead lease. The sidecar status must expose the candidate bead ID, phase=resolving, refreshed last_activity_at, and provider-child metadata before any claim or lease exists.

NON-SCOPE
Do not claim the parent before decomposition, change decomposition prompts or AC-map validation, change queue ordering, or make preclaim model work count as an implementation attempt. Do not kill provider processes that predate the hook baseline or belong to another worker.

PARENT
Split from root reliability regression bead: work: surface, bound, and reap preclaim decomposition lifecycle.

DEPS
ddx-aa6cc320 must land first so the internal/agent verification gate does not launch five real Codex providers.

acceptance:
1. TestOfflineJournal_SafeCompaction verifies compaction never drops an unacknowledged mutation.
2. TestOfflineJournal_SafeCompaction verifies compacted and reopened journals preserve unacknowledged mutations in original order with idempotency key, payload hash, precondition, and outcome fields intact.
3. TestOfflineJournal_SafeCompaction verifies acknowledged-through state and next monotonic sequence number survive compaction and close/reopen.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestOfflineJournal_SafeCompaction" passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Spec-first development · Executable specifications · Audit trail required · Work is a DAG · Drift is debt · Inspect and adapt</principles>
<concerns>go-std | security-owasp | testing</concerns>
<practices>Go domain interface with contract tests against real implementations · project-scoped durable files · no call-recording mocks · go test + lefthook</practices>
<adrs>ADR-022 rev 6 requires one reconnecting coordination client, durable ordered offline journal, idempotency keys, and a cross-process offline project lock.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-006 Worker Contract: connected mutations use the server; disconnected manual workers apply the same operations locally and reconcile before new online writes.</governing>
</context-digest>

PROBLEM
The offline coordination journal needs bounded durable storage, but compaction can corrupt reconciliation if it drops unacknowledged mutations or strips idempotency keys, payload hashes, preconditions, outcomes, sequence order, or acknowledgement state.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/workerprobe/probe.go:129-224 has no durable ordered mutation journal or compaction boundary. cli/cmd/try.go:256-259 and cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:280-282 construct local landing coordination directly, so there is no tested journal compaction behavior to preserve unacknowledged offline mutations before later online reconciliation.

PROPOSED FIX
Add safe compaction to the offline journal under cli/internal/agent. Compaction may remove acknowledged records at or below the durable acknowledgement-through sequence but must preserve all unacknowledged records in original order, keep their idempotency key, payload hash, precondition, and outcome fields, and retain enough state to continue monotonic sequence numbering after reopen.

NON-SCOPE
Do not add server routes, wire Cobra commands, centralize bead picking, or change process ownership. Do not implement the HTTP coordination client. Do not wire Cobra command flows to the journal in this bead beyond package-level production APIs and tests.

PARENT
- ddx-635ae251.

DEPS
- No deps.
title: agent: add offline coordination journal safe compaction

acceptance:
1. TestVerificationMappingParser passes: a fixture declaring requirement IDs yields those IDs in order; a fixture declaring none yields its section anchors as the inventory; a well-formed Verification table yields rows each exposing requirement ref, evidence target, and command; and rows missing a requirement ref, evidence target, or command each produce a parse-level finding.
2. The parser is read-only: TestVerificationMappingParser asserts no fixture file is modified.
3. cd cli && go test ./tools/lint/spechonesty/... passes.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Before coverage can be enforced, the analyzer must know, for any Complete/Implemented document, (a) the set of normative requirement IDs it declares (or, when it declares none, its stable section anchors) and (b) the rows of its Verification mapping table. Today none of this is parsed, so downstream coverage logic has nothing structured to check against.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The spechonesty analyzer at cli/tools/lint/spechonesty/analyzer.go (created by the scaffold/status-parser child) parses only status; it has no requirement-inventory or Verification-mapping parser. The contract is docs/helix/06-iterate/phase2-doc-truth-plan-2026-07-13.md WB-1 step 3.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a read-only pass that, for each parsed document, produces (1) an ordered list of normative requirement IDs, falling back to stable section anchors when the document declares no requirement IDs, and (2) a list of Verification mapping rows, each row exposing its requirement/anchor reference, its evidence target string (a `Test*` symbol, a named static check, or an inspectable runtime artifact path), and its executable verification command string. Emit a structured model only; do not resolve targets, do not judge coverage, do not mutate any file. Malformed rows (missing requirement ref, missing evidence target, or missing command) must be surfaced as parse-level findings so later stages can rely on well-formed rows.

NON-SCOPE
Do not resolve whether a named test/check/artifact exists — that is the coverage child. Do not enforce one-mapping-per-requirement or zero-evidence rejection. Do not handle waivers. Do not auto-restamp documents, infer proof from prose, fetch network state, or mark aspirational metrics complete. Status parsing lives in the scaffold child. Duplicate-id and stale-observation detection are a sibling child. Analyzer remains read-only.

DEPS: the spechonesty scaffold/status-parser child (provides the package and status parser). Sibling coverage and waiver children depend on this child's model.
title: spechonesty lint: parse normative requirement inventory and Verification mapping rows into a structured model

acceptance:
1. TestValidateAttemptIntegrity_MissingRequiredGateEvidenceRejected proves a code-changing attempt whose bead requires lefthook is rejected when GateRuns is empty, failed, interrupted, background-only, or no-staged-files.
2. TestValidateAttemptIntegrity_NoVerifyImplementationCommitRejected proves an implementation-agent git commit --no-verify command rejects the attempt even when another gate run appears meaningful.
3. TestValidateAttemptIntegrity_AllowsDDXOwnedNoVerifyCommits proves scoped checkpoint, tracker-only, landing, and durable-audit commits remain permitted because they are outside implementation-agent evidence.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "TestValidateAttemptIntegrity_(MissingRequiredGateEvidenceRejected|NoVerifyImplementationCommitRejected|AllowsDDXOwnedNoVerifyCommits)$" -count=1 -timeout 120s passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
A code-changing execute-bead attempt can bypass required staged hooks and still be accepted. Attempt 20260713T094206-53684645 for ddx-415c8b95 committed with git commit --no-verify after its pre-commit run was interrupted; DDx merged and closed it.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_validation.go:160-186 validates gate evidence only when GateRuns is non-empty, while cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1507-1514 calls ValidateAttemptIntegrity without evidence. The validator therefore treats missing evidence as success and does not distinguish implementation-agent bypass commands from orchestrator-owned commits.

PROPOSED FIX
Make the attempt-integrity validator fail closed when a code-changing bead requires a staged lefthook run but meaningful successful evidence is absent. Reject implementation-agent commit commands that use --no-verify. Explicitly retain the scoped exception for DDx-owned checkpoint, tracker-only, landing, and durable-audit commits, which are outside implementation-agent evidence.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_validation.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_validation_test.go

NON-SCOPE
Do not wire live harness evidence or change execute-bead prompts; ddx-03568f89 owns that integration. Do not rewrite existing execution history or forbid DDx-owned orchestration commits.

PARENT
ddx-9c3e703b.

DEPS
None.

3. TestNoChangesBlocked_PersistsLocalResourceBlockerKind proves applyNoChangesBlockedExternal writes the typed Extra marker and preserves the operator-readable reason.
4. The production execute-bead prompt documents blocker_kind: local_resource_exhaustion and its narrow applicability.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/try/... ./internal/agent/... -run "Test(ParseNoChangesRationale_(LocalResourceBlockerKind|RejectsUnknownBlockerKind)|NoChangesBlocked_PersistsLocalResourceBlockerKind)" passes.

description:
PROBLEM
When an execution agent cannot start its shell because the host temp root is exhausted, it can only return status: blocked plus prose. DDx then parks the bead indefinitely even after resource health recovers.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:35-98 parses status, reason, suggested_action, and retry fields but has no machine-readable blocker kind. cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:193-203 carries no typed blocker field in NoChangesOutcome. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:6605-6626 persists only ExternalBlockerReason. The execution prompt at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2046 documents blocked prose but no typed local-resource contract.

PROPOSED FIX
- Accept optional blocker_kind: local_resource_exhaustion only with status: blocked; reject unknown kinds and invalid status combinations.
- Carry the parsed kind through NoChangesOutcome and persist the typed marker defined by ddx-89ee92b8 in applyNoChangesBlockedExternal.
- Update the execution prompt so agents use the field only for host-local byte/inode/writability exhaustion, not test failures or missing dependencies.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go
- nearest focused *_test.go files

NON-SCOPE
- Do not parse ENOSPC from arbitrary reason prose.
- Do not perform the recovery probe or reopen beads.
- Do not classify provider, routing, or acceptance failures as local resources.

PARENT
- ddx-5958e8a4.

DEPS
- ddx-89ee92b8 supplies the typed marker schema.
title: agent: persist typed local resource blockers from blocked outcomes

description:
PROBLEM
The same regression can leave fixture-owned subprocesses running after a failure or timeout, which hides hangs and leaks provider descendants outside the test lifecycle.
ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_concurrent_predispatch_test.go:35-55` starts the fixture, but there is no explicit assertion that a canceled context waits for or kills its children. `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:102-113` can produce the provider-launch PATH shim descendants that must be reaped.
PROPOSED FIX
Add a failure/timeout-path test in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_concurrent_predispatch_test.go` that cancels the fixture context, waits for exit, and asserts every descendant is either waited on or killed before the test returns. Include a process-table check for lingering children that reference the test temp root.
NON-SCOPE
Changing provider discovery, routing policy, or the spawn-tripwire assertions; modifying unrelated agent tests.
PARENT
`ddx-aa6cc320`. DEPS None.
notes: The regression now uses a hermetic in-process AgentRunner and owns no subprocess to reap; the proposed cleanup assertion became vacuous.
title: Prove subprocess cancellation and reaping on regression failure

acceptance:
1. TestWorkLoop_PreClaimDecompositionReapsProviderDescendants covers success, deterministic fallback, error, timeout, and cancellation; it proves hook-created child process groups are terminated and reaped while a provider process present in the pre-hook baseline survives.
2. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run TestWorkLoop_PreClaimDecompositionReapsProviderDescendants -count=1 passes.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... passes.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
After the ddx-29491a0e preclaim decomposer returned on 2026-07-13, an interactive nested `codex --no-alt-screen` process (PID 607041, process group 607041) remained directly under the DDx worker until the operator terminated it. Preclaim providers can therefore leak CPU after successful fallback or other hook exits.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3108-3120 invokes PostAttemptDecompositionHook before claim without wrapping the hook in provider-descendant cleanup. Attempt-end descendant reaping in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1150-1163 is never reached because decomposition happens before an implementation attempt exists.

PROPOSED FIX
Establish a preclaim lifecycle process baseline immediately before invoking the decomposition hook and reap every provider descendant introduced by the hook on success, deterministic fallback, error, timeout, and cancellation. Preserve any provider process present in the pre-hook baseline and any process owned by another worker.

NON-SCOPE
Do not claim the parent before decomposition, change decomposition prompts or AC-map validation, change queue ordering, or make preclaim model work count as an implementation attempt. Do not kill provider processes that predate the hook baseline or belong to another worker.

PARENT
Split from root reliability regression bead: work: surface, bound, and reap preclaim decomposition lifecycle.

DEPS
ddx-aa6cc320 must land first so the internal/agent verification gate does not launch five real Codex providers.

{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"declared dependency ddx-aa6cc320 must land before the full internal/agent suite (ac #3) is safe to run, per the bead's own deps note about real codex provider launches: Bead prose meets the authoring rubric well: title is imperative, PROBLEM/ROOT CAUSE (with file:line at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3108-3120 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1150-1163)/PROPOSED FIX/NON-SCOPE are all present, AC names a specific Test* function and go test -run filter, labels carry cross-refs, and parent+deps are explicit. However, this exact bead has failed 5 consecutive dispatch attempts (2026-07-16T22:32 through 2026-07-17T09:50), each recorded as 'session closed before Stop hook' with failure_class unknown, spanning both sonnet-4.6 (standard) and opus-4.7 (readiness-escalated) routes. Since escalating model power did not change the outcome, the failure is not a capability problem but an environment/dependency problem. The bead's own DEPS line states ddx-aa6cc320 must land first 'so the internal/agent verification gate does not launch five real Codex providers' during `go test ./internal/agent/...` (AC #3) — if that dependency has not actually landed yet, every attempt's mandated full-package test run would trigger real provider launches, plausibly explaining repeated session death before the Stop hook fires. acceptance:
1. TestExecutionCleanupFixtures_UseHermeticRoots proves every audited internal/agent fixture invoking production `ExecutionCleanupManager` keeps effective temp and scratch roots beneath its own `t.TempDir()`.
2. TestExecutionCleanup_ReclaimsExpiredTestOwnedWorktrees uses private fixture/config roots and completes without enumerating a host-global DDx scratch tree or module cache.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "Test(ExecutionCleanupFixtures_UseHermeticRoots|ExecutionCleanup_ReclaimsExpiredTestOwnedWorktrees)$" -count=1 -timeout 120s` passes.
4. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The cleanup helper coverage in `execution_cleanup_test.go` is not yet enforcing that every production `ExecutionCleanupManager.Cleanup` fixture keeps its roots beneath its own `t.TempDir()`.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_test.go:102-114 centralizes project-root setup but does not assert hermetic effective cleanup roots for all internal/agent cleanup fixtures.
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process_test.go and cli/internal/agent/execution_resources_test.go invoke production cleanup paths that can inherit shared roots if call sites are missed.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a shared fixture audit helper that checks each internal/agent cleanup fixture using production `ExecutionCleanupManager.Cleanup` resolves temp and scratch roots under its own `t.TempDir()`, then update the audited call sites to pass explicit local roots.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change cleanup behavior, task scheduling, or resource deletion semantics. Do not add new production flags or package-level global fixtures.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-e6abf4a6. Deps: none. Supersedes the execution scope of ddx-12a41acc, which remains as failed-attempt audit only.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestExecutionCleanupFixtures_UseHermeticRoots proves every audited internal/agent fixture invoking production `ExecutionCleanupManager` keeps effective temp and scratch roots beneath its own `t.TempDir()`.
2. TestExecutionCleanup_ReclaimsExpiredTestOwnedWorktrees uses private fixture/config roots and completes without enumerating a host-global DDx scratch tree or module cache.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "Test(ExecutionCleanupFixtures_UseHermeticRoots|ExecutionCleanup_ReclaimsExpiredTestOwnedWorktrees)$" -count=1 -timeout 120s` passes.
4. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
notes: Tracker parent metadata ddx-34113f35 is authoritative; the generated PARENT AND DEPS prose saying ddx-e6abf4a6 reflects the grandparent. This focused fixture audit may execute independently of ddx-427370dc because it owns separate cleanup-manager call sites.
title: Audit internal/agent cleanup fixtures for hermetic roots

acceptance:
1. Add or update a worker-manager shutdown regression so a live managed worker with descendant git/provider subprocesses is reaped through the existing worker shutdown path even when supervisor cancellation is slow.
2. `Server.Shutdown()` must not block indefinitely on `supervisorDone`; timeout behavior must return/log a useful error while continuing to close bead hub and stop land coordinators.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-24949caf is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM: Restarting the DDx user service during active supervised work can hang until systemd times out, leaving systemd to SIGKILL server child processes. Live evidence from 2026-07-07 03:05:19 EDT: `ddx server stop` did not return until systemd marked `ddx-server.service` failed with `Result: timeout` and killed leftover git subprocesses, interrupting the Axon worker attempt that was in post-execution handling.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: `cli/internal/server/server.go:379-385` cancels the supervisor and then waits on `supervisorDone` with no timeout. Worker cleanup is only reached afterward at `cli/internal/server/server.go:388-395`. The worker shutdown path itself has a bounded wait (`cli/internal/server/workers.go:1696-1703`), but it is unreachable while the supervisor goroutine is stuck in a reconcile/git operation. The installed systemd unit generated from `cli/cmd/server_service.go:26-42` has no explicit stop timeout or ExecStop mitigation, so the default systemd timeout eventually kills the whole cgroup.

PROPOSED FIX: Make server shutdown bounded and ordered so worker cleanup always runs promptly even when supervisor reconciliation is slow or stuck. A good implementation is to cancel the supervisor, wait only a short bounded grace for `supervisorDone`, continue to `supervisorRegistry.Shutdown()` / `workers.Shutdown()` on timeout, and surface/log the supervisor timeout without preventing worker cleanup.

NON-SCOPE: Do not rewrite worker execution semantics, change bead claim/lease policy, or alter unrelated service install behavior.

Parent: none. Dependencies: none.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Add or update a worker-manager shutdown regression so a live managed worker with descendant git/provider subprocesses is reaped through the existing worker shutdown path even when supervisor cancellation is slow.
- `Server.Shutdown()` must not block indefinitely on `supervisorDone`; timeout behavior must return/log a useful error while continuing to close bead hub and stop land coordinators.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

acceptance:
1. TestFizeauExecuteCancellationTerminatesWrappedProcessTree proves cancellation through the pinned public Execute context leaves no harness/provider descendants.
2. TestFizeauCallerDeathTerminatesWrappedProcessTree kills the embedding DDx worker and proves Fizeau containment leaves no provider tree.
3. TestDDXNeverInstallsProviderShimOrLaunchWrapper proves production DDx contains no provider PATH shim, __provider-launch command, concrete provider-binary wrapper, or PR_SET_PDEATHSIG provider launcher.
4. TestDDXNeverScansOrSignalsFizeauOwnedProcesses proves production DDx contains no provider-child/orphan-harness census, route-aware child liveness, or signal/reap call keyed by harness/provider/model.
5. TestGenericCleanupTargetsOnlyDDXOwnedProcesses proves attempt/worker/container cleanup still terminates DDx-owned processes while ignoring opaque Fizeau session internals.
6. TestNoProviderProcessOwnershipSymbols AST-checks production for installProviderShimOnPATH, ProviderLaunchSubcommand, scanProviderChildrenForStatus, runningProviderChildGuard, reapSupersededProviderChildren, reapAllProviderChildren, orphanHarnessReaper, and provider-descendant cleanup branches.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... ./cmd/... -run 'Test(FizeauExecuteCancellationTerminatesWrappedProcessTree|FizeauCallerDeathTerminatesWrappedProcessTree|DDXNeverInstallsProviderShimOrLaunchWrapper|DDXNeverScansOrSignalsFizeauOwnedProcesses|GenericCleanupTargetsOnlyDDXOwnedProcesses|NoProviderProcessOwnershipSymbols)' -count=1 -timeout 300s passes.
8. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... ./cmd/... -count=1 -timeout 600s passes.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
DDx still installs provider-binary PATH shims, exposes a hidden provider-launch wrapper, scans concrete harness process trees, and signals/reaps Fizeau-owned children. Fizeau v0.15 containment work is implemented upstream but not yet published or pinned, so DDx cannot yet delete the safety fallback. Without a dedicated consumer bead, publishing v0.15 would leave this forbidden boundary crossing in DDx indefinitely.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:93-102,116-128,276-297 installs the provider shim and performs route-aware child cleanup around Fizeau Execute.
- cli/internal/agent/provider_spawn.go:13-56 and 100-179 defines the hidden wrapper contract and concrete provider binary shims.
- cli/cmd/provider_launch.go:12-70 plus provider_launch platform files exposes the direct provider exec boundary.
- cli/internal/agent/provider_children.go:21-40,92-240,242-478 discovers, classifies, signals, and reaps provider children using concrete route/harness facts.
- cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper.go:42-169 plus orphan_harness_reaper_linux.go:22-113 discovers and kills orphan harness processes.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1249-1258 and execute_bead_loop.go:1616-1618 retain attempt-end and liveness integration.
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process.go:61-195 and cli/internal/server/workers_reap_unix.go:33-213 mix Fizeau-owned descendants into otherwise DDx-owned attempt/worker cleanup.
- Fizeau containment epic fizeau-c795e7d9 is closed, but release bead fizeau-b9a2037c must publish and prove cancellation/caller-death cleanup before DDx removes the fallback.

PROPOSED FIX
After pinning the compatible Fizeau release, add consumer conformance proving Execute cancellation and caller death terminate every wrapped harness/provider process tree. Delete the PATH shim, hidden provider-launch command/platform implementation, concrete provider binary list, provider-child/orphan-harness census and reapers, route-aware child liveness, attempt-end provider reap, and provider-descendant branches in generic cleanup. DDx retains only cancellation of the context supplied to Fizeau Execute and may supervise/reap DDx-owned worker, attempt, verification, and container processes. No concrete returned route fact may authorize a signal.

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken DDx-owned worker/attempt/container cleanup, move bead/worktree/verification/landing ownership to Fizeau, parse terminal text, or implement the typed outcome adapter owned by ddx-66a8883f.

PARENT
ddx-b8cacbeb.

DEPS
fizeau#fizeau-b9a2037c publishes the process-tree containment contract and is the structured external blocker. fizeau#fizeau-c795e7d9 supplies the already-landed implementation.
notes: Fizeau v0.15 pin and public conformance are centralized in ddx-00d406ce; this consumer must not repin Fizeau independently.
title: execution: delete DDx supervision of Fizeau-owned provider processes

TestExecuteBeadLoop_RepeatedBestEffortReadinessErrorsDoNotStopDrain creates more than DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold ready beads whose pre-claim hook returns the same nonfatal intake error; it proves all beads execute/close, Run has no terminal OperatorAttention, and a durable non-terminal operator-attention event is emitted exactly once.
2. TestPreClaimWarnSameFingerprintEscalatesAfterThreshold or its successor still proves a claim-blocking system_unready policy stops before the threshold bead.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "Test(ExecuteBeadLoop_RepeatedBestEffortReadinessErrorsDoNotStopDrain|PreClaimWarnSameFingerprintEscalatesAfterThreshold)" -count=1 passes.
