description:
PROBLEM
A DDx watch worker exits after five distinct beads when the best-effort readiness hook repeatedly returns system_unready, even though each failure is explicitly logged as continuing and the worker can still claim and execute beads. This converts an advisory Fizeau readiness outage into a queue-wide drain stop.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3010-3058 logs intakeErr as "check unavailable ... (continuing)" but passes it to appendPreClaimWarn. execute_bead_loop.go:1961-1984 makes the fifth matching warning set OperatorAttention and terminate Run. The observed watch run exited after ddx-029c1dde, ddx-744b0996, ddx-b0de0d36, and ddx-8c8675ba with preclaim_warn_repeated while all failures were non-blocking readiness-route JSON errors.

PROPOSED FIX
For fail-open best-effort readiness intake errors, retain durable warning/operator-attention observability but do not return Stop from watch/drain execution. Keep terminal behavior for claim-blocking readiness policy, project corruption, and other genuine safety stops. Add focused regression proof using a repeated intakeErr across more than DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold beads that confirms the worker completes the available queue and records non-terminal attention.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change Fizeau routing, harness selection, failure-class vocabulary, readiness policy when configured blocking, or bead execution escalation.
title: agent: keep watch drain alive on repeated best-effort readiness outages

acceptance:
1. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "^(TestProviderShimExecutableResolver_RejectsGoTestBinary|TestRunAgentViaService_UsesProviderShimExecutableResolver|TestDispatchViaResolvedConfig_UsesProviderShimExecutableResolver|TestAgentPackageSuite_DoesNotExecTestBinaryAsProviderLauncher|TestExecutor_ProviderSpawnSetsPdeathsigAndSetpgid)$" -count=1 -timeout 120s` passes.
2. `cd cli && go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./internal/agent` passes without any `agent.test __provider-launch` descendant, orphaned real provider, or recursive package-test process.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-b291b0f6 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
The mandatory `cd cli && go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./internal/agent` gate recursively launches the package test binary as DDx's hidden provider wrapper. During attempt `20260713T190502-df5adc46`, the parent `agent.test` spawned five concurrent `agent.test __provider-launch <real-provider>` helpers for more than three minutes, grew a 666 MB testlog, and left a real `codex --no-alt-screen` process reparented to the managed worker for 12 minutes at about 188 MiB RSS. This makes the package gate itself create the CPU/memory/orphan condition it is supposed to prevent.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:102-113` calls `os.Executable()` unconditionally and passes that path to `EnsureProviderShimOnPATH`.
- `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:152-163` repeats the same unconditional installation for the resolved-service path.
- `cli/internal/agent/provider_spawn.go:83-118` writes each shim as `<ddxBinary> __provider-launch <realBinary>`. Under `go test`, `os.Executable()` is `agent.test`, not the DDx CLI and it has no Cobra `__provider-launch` dispatch. Re-executing it starts the test suite again; the recursive process can install shims and launch real providers that escape the original gate process group.
- `cli/cmd/provider_launch.go:25-68` is the actual hidden-command implementation, and exists only in the DDx CLI command graph.

PROPOSED FIX
Introduce one injectable resolver for the provider-wrapper executable and use it from both service call sites. In package-test builds, install a suite-wide test resolver/guard that refuses to use the current `agent.test` executable as a provider wrapper while leaving direct `EnsureProviderShimOnPATH` tests free to supply an explicit real/fake DDx CLI binary. Fail closed with actionable evidence if a test fixture attempts to install a provider shim from a Go test binary. Preserve production PATH shim, `Pdeathsig`, process-group, and real CLI hidden-command behavior.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not remove provider parent-death protection in production.
- Do not replace Fizeau routing or provider discovery.
- Do not weaken the full `internal/agent` race gate or merely increase its timeout.
- Do not kill unrelated provider processes from other repositories.
- Scanner injection remains owned by `ddx-02200b1e`; fixture-specific spawn tripwires remain owned by `ddx-26bf19c7`.

PARENT AND DEPS
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "^(TestProviderShimExecutableResolver_RejectsGoTestBinary|TestRunAgentViaService_UsesProviderShimExecutableResolver|TestDispatchViaResolvedConfig_UsesProviderShimExecutableResolver|TestAgentPackageSuite_DoesNotExecTestBinaryAsProviderLauncher|TestExecutor_ProviderSpawnSetsPdeathsigAndSetpgid)$" -count=1 -timeout 120s` passes.
- `cd cli && go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./internal/agent` passes without any `agent.test __provider-launch` descendant, orphaned real provider, or recursive package-test process.
- `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
notes: Cancelled: deterministic lifecycle fallback produced a verification-only child with no implementation dependency wiring. Its gate scope was consolidated atomically into ddx-5653688f.
title: agent tests: prevent provider shim from re-executing Go test binaries: part 3

acceptance:
1. `TestExecuteLoop_TwoWorkersBothClaimP0sBeforeP2s_UsesHermeticProcessScanner` proves the two-worker fixture uses the bounded seam while preserving its queue assertions.
2. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerEndToEndThreeBeadDrain_UsesHermeticProcessScanner` proves the end-to-end three-bead drain fixture uses the bounded seam while preserving its queue assertions.
3. `cd cli && go test -race ./internal/agent -run '^(TestExecuteLoop_TwoWorkersBothClaimP0sBeforeP2s(_UsesHermeticProcessScanner)?|TestExecuteBeadWorkerEndToEndThreeBeadDrain(_UsesHermeticProcessScanner)?)$' -count=1 -timeout 60s` passes.
4. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The default-suite worker-loop fixtures still enter the real startup path, so the host-wide scanner is exercised during tests that only need queue behavior.
ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_priority_test.go:87-188` and the `TestExecuteBeadWorkerEndToEndThreeBeadDrain` fixture invoke startup without a hermetic process-scanner seam, while `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1685-1700` currently wires the scanner unconditionally.
PROPOSED FIX
Update the default-suite worker-loop fixtures to inject the bounded scanner seam, keep their queue assertions unchanged, and leave the production reaper path untouched for recovery-focused tests.
NON-SCOPE
Changing fixture assertions, queue ordering semantics, Linux `/proc` coverage, or orphan recovery policy.
PARENT
Discovered while validating the Niflheim provider-test CPU regression parent.
DEPS
None.

acceptance:
1. TestWorkLoop_PreClaimDecompositionClearsTransientLiveness covers success, fallback/error, and cancellation and proves no stale candidate/phase remains after the hook returns.
2. TestWorkLoop_PreClaimDecompositionPreservesLoopStartOrdering proves loop.start remains the first structured event even though preclaim liveness now emits before claim.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestWorkLoop_PreClaimDecomposition(ClearsTransientLiveness|PreservesLoopStartOrdering)" -count=1 passes.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Once preclaim decomposition starts publishing resolving liveness, stale candidate state could make `ddx work status --json` report old bead IDs or phases after the hook exits. The existing loop.start structured event ordering must also remain stable so downstream execution telemetry does not regress.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1604-1617 defers liveness emission until claim, so there is currently no preclaim transient state lifecycle to clear. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3108-3120 invokes PostAttemptDecompositionHook before claim, so adding liveness there can accidentally emit before the existing loop.start event unless the new lifecycle is sequenced explicitly.

PROPOSED FIX
Clear transient candidate resolving state on every preclaim decomposition exit, including success, deterministic fallback/error, timeout, and cancellation. Sequence the new preclaim liveness setup so the existing loop.start structured event remains the first structured event while still allowing resolving liveness before claim.

NON-SCOPE
Do not claim the parent before decomposition, change decomposition prompts or AC-map validation, change queue ordering, or make preclaim model work count as an implementation attempt. Do not kill provider processes that predate the hook baseline or belong to another worker.

PARENT
Split from root reliability regression bead: work: surface, bound, and reap preclaim decomposition lifecycle.

DEPS
ddx-aa6cc320 must land first so the internal/agent verification gate does not launch five real Codex providers.

acceptance:
1. TestExecutionCleanup_BoundsLargeScratchTree creates a high-cardinality fixture tree and proves one cleanup pass returns within the configured budget with a structured deferred-cleanup observation.
2. TestExecutionCleanup_CancelStopsMeasurementAndRemoval cancels during measurement and during deletion and proves both phases return promptly without continuing background mutation.
3. TestWorkCleanup_DoesNotBlockClaimOnLargeScratchTree proves the worker reaches the next candidate after the cleanup budget expires and later passes resume safe reclamation.
4. Tests prove live, foreign non-test-owned, registered, fresh, and metadata-preserved scratch paths remain untouched across budget boundaries.
5. Production defaults cap synchronous cleanup work without disabling eventual reclamation, and operator-visible summary fields distinguish reclaimed from deferred work.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "Test(ExecutionCleanup_(BoundsLargeScratchTree|CancelStopsMeasurementAndRemoval)|WorkCleanup_DoesNotBlockClaimOnLargeScratchTree)$" -count=1 -timeout 60s passes.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
A ddx work worker can spend minutes synchronously measuring and deleting one accumulated scratch tree before it claims useful work. During the 2026-07-13 niflheim incident, one cleanup pass performed hundreds of megabytes of physical reads; the same path timed out at both 120 seconds in isolation and 600 seconds in the full package gate. This creates unbounded CPU/I/O latency and makes queue progress inversely proportional to stale cache size.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1687 runs the execution cleanup pass on the claim path. cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:620-747 scans every matching scratch directory, calls measureTreeWithContext over an unbounded tree, and then calls os.RemoveAll, which walks the tree again and cannot observe context cancellation. cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:830-843 includes the shared cache parent and legacy process temp directory by default. A large stale directory therefore monopolizes the single queue worker with no per-pass item, inode, byte, or wall-clock budget.

PROPOSED FIX
- Put a deterministic wall-clock/work budget around scratch discovery, measurement, and reclamation on worker passes.
- Make cancellation effective during both measurement and deletion; do not enter an uninterruptible whole-tree RemoveAll after the budget expires.
- Persist or rediscover unfinished candidates safely so later passes continue reclamation without restarting an O(tree) measurement from zero.
- Return a non-fatal structured deferred-cleanup observation when the budget is exhausted, then allow candidate selection to proceed.
- Preserve liveness, ownership, registered-worktree, and minimum-age checks before any mutation.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go only as needed for the bounded pass contract
- focused cleanup tests

NON-SCOPE
Do not delete live worktrees, reduce the six-hour minimum age, disable cleanup, or treat cleanup deferral as bead failure.

PARENT
None; root production reliability regression.

DEPS
Depends on ddx-e6abf4a6 so the hermetic test boundary lands before production cleanup stress tests run.
title: cleanup: bound scratch reclamation work per worker pass

acceptance:
1. `TestRunnerEmbeddedDispatchHomesDoNotUsePrivateGoModuleCache` exercises the LM Studio and OpenRouter embedded-dispatch fixtures and proves their isolated HOME never gains `go/pkg/mod`.
2. `TestRunnerEmbeddedDispatchHomesDoNotCreateGlobalDDXHome` snapshots `os.TempDir` before and after the fixture and proves no new `ddx-home-*` directory is created.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "TestRunnerEmbeddedDispatchHomesDo(NotUsePrivateGoModuleCache|NotCreateGlobalDDXHome)$" -count=1 -timeout 30s` passes.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The embedded-dispatch runner tests currently create isolated HOME directories with os.MkdirTemp and then change HOME. On Niflheim, an abandoned ddx-home-* directory accumulated a large private Go module cache, and later test gates recursively traversed that scratch tree. The gap is not just fixture cleanup; there is no regression test proving the isolated HOME stays free of go/pkg/mod and does not mint a fresh process-global ddx-home-* under os.TempDir.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go:29-34 and cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go:56-61 create the raw HOME fixture and discard cleanup errors, which makes it possible for Go subprocess defaults to spill into the temp tree and for abandoned ddx-home-* directories to persist undetected.

PROPOSED FIX
Add tripwire tests in the runner test file that exercise both embedded-dispatch paths, snapshot os.TempDir before and after the fixture, and prove no new ddx-home-* directory is created. Also assert the isolated HOME never gains go/pkg/mod. These tests should fail if the fixture pattern regresses.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change production runner routing, make live provider calls, delete existing host scratch, or change production cleanup ownership/budgets. ddx-f914dddd and ddx-de1ff4b4 own those cleanup changes.

PARENT
ddx-6a2ce3bd.

DEPS
None.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go

1. `TestRunnerEmbeddedDispatchHomesDoNotUsePrivateGoModuleCache` exercises the LM Studio and OpenRouter embedded-dispatch fixtures and proves their isolated HOME never gains `go/pkg/mod`.
2. `TestRunnerEmbeddedDispatchHomesDoNotCreateGlobalDDXHome` snapshots `os.TempDir` before and after the fixture and proves no new `ddx-home-*` directory is created.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "TestRunnerEmbeddedDispatchHomesDo(NotUsePrivateGoModuleCache|NotCreateGlobalDDXHome)$" -count=1 -timeout 30s` passes.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
title: Add tripwire tests that detect private Go module caches and global ddx-home directories

acceptance:
1. TestDuplicateDocumentIDFails passes: two documents sharing a document id (TD-040 class) produce a failure that no waiver suppresses.
2. TestDuplicateUserStoryIDFails passes: the same US-id appearing in two features (US-087/US-088 class) produces a failure that no waiver suppresses.
3. TestStaleObservationFails passes against an injected fixture clock/revision: a Complete Verification row whose observation revision differs from the current revision fails, while a current-revision exit-zero observation passes.
4. cd cli && go test ./tools/lint/spechonesty/... passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Duplicate document ids (TD-040 class) and duplicate user-story ids across features (US-087/US-088 collision class) pass unnoticed, and a Complete document can cite a verification observation captured at a stale repository revision rather than the current one, so a checked-in assertion that a command once passed is treated as current proof.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The spechonesty analyzer at cli/tools/lint/spechonesty/analyzer.go (from the scaffold child) has no duplicate-id or observation-freshness logic. The contract is docs/helix/06-iterate/phase2-doc-truth-plan-2026-07-13.md WB-1 step 4 (current-revision observation) and step 6 (duplicate ids), scoped to features/ plus all five 02-design subdirectories.

PROPOSED FIX
Add two independent checks. (1) Duplicate-id detection: fail on duplicate document ids and on duplicate US-ids across features, scoping the scan to features/ plus the five 02-design subdirectories; these failures are never suppressible by a waiver. (2) Stale-observation detection: a Complete/Implemented Verification mapping row passes only when its recorded observation names the current repository revision with exit-zero; an observation keyed to a stale revision is a failure. Both checks are deterministic and read-only; the current revision and any fixture clock are injected so tests are hermetic (do not fetch network state).

NON-SCOPE
Do not auto-restamp documents, infer proof from prose, fetch network state, or mark aspirational metrics complete. Complete-coverage/waiver logic and status parsing live in sibling children. Prose quality and AC numbering style are out of scope.

PARENT: ddx-2e992616. DEPS: the spechonesty scaffold/status-parser child (provides the package and status parser).

title: spechonesty lint: detect duplicate document/US ids and stale-revision verification observations
work-last-detail: session closed before Stop hook

acceptance:
1. A parent/superseded bead with an unclosed blocking dependency is NOT auto-closed by the roll-up path; `cascadeAndWalkUp` skips it and the bead stays open. Covered by `TestRollUpClose_RefusesBeadWithOpenBlocksDep` in cli/internal/bead/store_test.go, reproducing the pqueue-842ac11f shape: bead A blocks-depends-on B (in_progress), a roll-up close is triggered, and the test asserts A is still open afterward.
2. The skip records an auditable note/event on the affected bead naming the open dep ID; asserted within `TestRollUpClose_RefusesBeadWithOpenBlocksDep`.
3. Roll-up still auto-closes eligible beads whose blocking deps are all closed (no regression to RC-2/RC-3 covered by existing TestClose_EpicAutoClose_* / TestClose_WalkUp_* / TestClose_SupersededCascade_Eligible); covered by new `TestRollUpClose_AllowsBeadWithClosedBlocksDep`.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... green`.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM: The 2026-07-06 pqueue incident: a server-managed worker performed a tracker-only roll-up close (no closing commit) on pqueue-842ac11f while it still carried an open 'blocks' dependency on pqueue-657e5f8d (in_progress). The programmatic auto-close paths do not validate dependency status, so a parent/superseded bead is auto-closed even when it has unmet blocking deps.

ROOT CAUSE: `Store.cascadeAndWalkUp` (cli/internal/bead/store.go:1661) runs auto-close paths that call `SetLifecycleStatus(..., StatusClosed, ...)` directly, bypassing dependency checks: (a) RC-2 cascade-close of superseded beads via `cascadeCloseSuperseeded` (store.go:1833), guarded only by `canCascadeCloseSuperseeded` (store.go:1867) which checks supersession/claim state but not deps; (b) RC-3 walk-up via `walkUpClosureCandidate` (store.go:1712) which auto-closes dead-intermediate (`execution-eligible==false`) and epic parents once children are terminal. Neither consults sibling dependency statuses. This is the exact path that produced the incident (tracker-only roll-up, no commit). Reconcile also closes programmatically at cli/internal/bead/reconcile.go:285 — out of scope here.

PROPOSED FIX: Depends on the sibling bead adding `ErrDependencyGateRejected` and `Store.UnclosedBlockingDeps(id)` (cli/internal/bead/errors.go, store.go). Before each auto-close `SetLifecycleStatus(x.ID, StatusClosed, ...)` in `cascadeCloseSuperseeded` (store.go:1833) and before the closed transition inside `walkUpClosureCandidate` (store.go:1712), call `s.UnclosedBlockingDeps(<candidateID>)`; if it returns a non-empty slice, SKIP that auto-close (leave the bead open), append an audit note/event naming the open dep IDs (mirroring `appendClosureRejectNote`, store.go:2079), and continue the walk without closing that node. The roll-up must not abort the whole operation — it selectively skips the ineligible bead. If the sibling primitive has not landed, add a minimal resolver inline at the roll-up call sites rather than duplicating the exported method.

NON-SCOPE: Do NOT change `Store.Close` / `Store.CloseWithEvidence` / `Store.UpdateWithLifecycleStatus` direct-close gating (sibling bead). Do NOT change claim paths (sibling bead). Do NOT change `Reconcile` (reconcile.go:285). Do NOT alter epic/dead-intermediate terminal-child counting beyond adding the dep-gate skip.

acceptance:
1. `TestLifecycleDiscoveryContainment_PreservesConcurrentUnownedProvider` starts an unrelated provider after the pre-call snapshot while `ListModels` is active and proves it survives while the current call-owned probe is reaped.
2. `TestLifecycleDiscoveryContainment_BoundsBaselineAndCleanupScans` injects blocked baseline and cleanup enumeration and proves each returns within the configured containment budget with no `context.Background()` wait.
3. `TestLifecycleDiscoveryContainment_SurfacesCleanupFailure` proves scan, signal, and reap failures are returned or durably recorded instead of being silently treated as successful containment.
4. `TestLifecycleModelDiscovery_CancellationReapsPTYProbe` and `TestLifecycleModelDiscovery_ReturnDoesNotLeaveNoAltScreenChild` remain green for success, upstream error, timeout, and cancellation.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent ./cmd -run "^(TestLifecycleDiscoveryContainment_(PreservesConcurrentUnownedProvider|BoundsBaselineAndCleanupScans|SurfacesCleanupFailure)|TestLifecycleModelDiscovery_(CancellationReapsPTYProbe|ReturnDoesNotLeaveNoAltScreenChild))$" -count=1 -timeout 60s` passes.
6. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Commit `020853315` added `ListModelsWithProbeContainment`, which fixes the immediate leaked-probe symptom but identifies ownership by taking a process-wide provider baseline and reaping every provider descendant that appears afterward. A concurrent legitimate provider started under the same worker after the baseline can therefore be killed as if it belonged to this discovery call. Both scans use `context.Background()`, each termination can wait independently, and cleanup scan/termination errors are discarded, so the containment path can itself wedge or report false success.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/model_discovery_containment.go:28-35` attributes all post-baseline provider descendants to one `ListModels` call without a call-owned process/session identity.
- `cli/internal/agent/model_discovery_containment.go:38-64` runs both baseline and cleanup through `context.Background()` and drops cleanup errors.
- `cli/internal/agent/provider_children_unix.go:18-58` enumerates the host through `ps` and flattens descendants without PPID, process-group, session, or launch-owner metadata needed to prove call ownership.

PROPOSED FIX
Use an exact call-owned provider/session handle from the Fizeau cancellation work in `ddx-a751a7b1` when available and remove the process-wide baseline sweep. If a local fallback is still required, retain ancestry/session/launch ownership, serialize ambiguous lifecycle discovery, bound baseline and cleanup enumeration with explicit deadlines, fail closed by preserving ambiguous processes, and surface cleanup failures in the returned error and execution evidence. Cleanup must never wait on `context.Background()` or kill a provider solely because it appeared after a PID snapshot.

NON-SCOPE
Do not disable model discovery, extend the caller timeout, kill manual provider sessions, kill active implementation harnesses or their fixtures, weaken attempt-end cleanup, or change route selection.

PARENT
`ddx-f84ee9a7` (Niflheim provider-test CPU incident).

DEPS
Related to `ddx-a751a7b1` for the preferred upstream call-owned cancellation handle and `ddx-44e89575` for provider ancestry ownership; no hard dependency so the bounded fail-closed fallback can land independently.

status=blocked
reason=> The Bash tool in this execution worker is non-functional due to host-level /tmp exhaustion: every invocation (including trivial commands like `true`, `echo hi`, `ls /tmp`, `df -h`) fails identically with: ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/-Users-erik-Projects--ddx-exec-wt--execute-bead-wt-ddx-e1e229f5-20260713T155819-ab3d0d3d' This is the harness's own execution sandbox failing to create its scratch directory under /tmp on the host — not a condition inside this repo's worktree, and not something a code change in this bead's scope can fix. Confirmed with 4 consecutive retries over multiple minutes to rule out a one-off blip. This bead's acceptance criteria (AC5, AC6, AC7) all require running `go test` and `lefthook run pre-commit` via Bash. Without a working Bash tool, no test can be run, no git commit can be made, and no verification evidence can be produced. Read/Edit/Write tools remain functional, but making unverified code changes to concurrency-sensitive lifecycle/reaping logic (cli/internal/agent/model_discovery_containment.go, provider_children_unix.go) without being able to run the existing test suite or the new containment tests would violate the "do not commit red code" / "cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence" requirements in the execute-bead instructions. This is the third occurrence of this exact failure signature on this host across separate ddx execute-bead sessions (ddx-a751a7b1, ddx-34113f35, and now ddx-e1e229f5), all on different, unrelated beads. The host /tmp condition needs cleanup (or the harness's scratch-dir allocation needs to target a path with free space) before any worker on this host can execute a bead that requires Bash. This is an infrastructure/host blocker external to the DDx codebase itself, not something resolvable within this bead's scope.
suggested_action=recheck the external blocker and move status to open when cleared

acceptance:
1. TestStatusParserRecognizesBodyAndFrontmatter passes: given fixtures with a `**Status:**` body line (with free-text qualifier), a frontmatter `status:` key, and a document with neither, the parser returns the normalized base status for the first two and flags the SD/TD/ADR with no status marker as a missing-status failure.
2. The package exposes a `cmd/spechonesty` entrypoint that accepts a docs directory argument and exits non-zero on any parse/missing-status failure, exit-zero otherwise; the analyzer performs no writes.
3. cd cli && go test ./tools/lint/spechonesty/... passes.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
There is no mechanical way to read a DDx document's status stamp, so downstream verification-coverage, duplicate-id, and stale-observation checks have nothing to build on. Status markers are heterogeneous: some docs use a body line `**Status:**` with free-text qualifiers, some use YAML frontmatter `status:`, and 40 of 56 design docs (including SD-013 and TD-027) have no status marker at all.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The package `cli/tools/lint/spechonesty/` does not exist. The intended pattern is the sibling analyzer at cli/tools/lint/evidencelint/analyzer.go with a CLI entrypoint at cli/tools/lint/evidencelint/cmd/evidencelint/main.go and testdata fixtures under cli/tools/lint/evidencelint/testdata/. The mechanical contract is defined in docs/helix/06-iterate/phase2-doc-truth-plan-2026-07-13.md under WB-1, steps 1-2.

PROPOSED FIX
Create `cli/tools/lint/spechonesty/` (analyzer.go, analyzer_test.go, cmd/spechonesty/main.go, testdata/ fixtures) modeled on evidencelint. Implement a status parser that (a) recognizes both encodings — a body `**Status:**` line and YAML frontmatter `status:` — and normalizes free-text qualifiers to a base status (e.g. `Complete`, `Implemented`, `Proposed`, `In Progress`, `Deferred`, `Aspirational`), and (b) treats a missing status on any SD/TD/ADR document as a lint failure requiring a stamp. This missing-status rule — not the Complete-check — is what catches SD-013 and TD-027. Parsing must be deterministic and read-only; the linter never mutates documents.

NON-SCOPE
Do not auto-restamp documents, infer proof from prose, fetch network state, or mark aspirational metrics complete. Do not implement verification-coverage, waiver, duplicate-id, or stale-observation logic here (later children). Prose quality and AC numbering style are out of scope.

PARENT: ddx-2e992616. DEPS: none — this child lands first as the package foundation; later children depend on it.

Future WB-1 work for verification coverage, waivers, duplicate IDs, stale observations, pre-commit, and CI remains explicitly out of scope as the parent requested."}

acceptance:
1. TestOfflineJournal_AppendRecordDurability verifies appended records survive close/reopen with original sequence number, idempotency key, payload hash, precondition, and outcome fields intact.
2. TestOfflineJournal_AppendRecordDurability verifies sequence numbers are monotonic across multiple appends before close.
3. TestOfflineJournal_AppendRecordDurability verifies reopening the journal continues numbering after the highest persisted sequence.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestOfflineJournal_AppendRecordDurability" passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Spec-first development · Executable specifications · Audit trail required · Work is a DAG · Drift is debt · Inspect and adapt</principles>
<concerns>go-std | security-owasp | testing</concerns>
<practices>Go domain interface with contract tests against real implementations · project-scoped durable files · no call-recording mocks · go test + lefthook</practices>
<adrs>ADR-022 rev 6 requires one reconnecting coordination client, durable ordered offline journal, idempotency keys, and a cross-process offline project lock.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-006 Worker Contract: connected mutations use the server; disconnected manual workers apply the same operations locally and reconcile before new online writes.</governing>
</context-digest>

PROBLEM
Disconnected/manual worker mutations have no durable append record API, so a process crash can lose sequence ordering, idempotency keys, payload hashes, preconditions, or outcomes required for later reconciliation before online writes.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/workerprobe/probe.go:129-224 buffers only workerprobe events in memory. cli/cmd/try.go:256-259 and cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:280-282 construct local landing coordination directly, so no package-level production API records idempotency-keyed offline coordination mutations durably before local execution proceeds.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a project-scoped offline journal package under cli/internal/agent that can create/open a durable journal file, append mutation records with monotonic sequence numbers, idempotency key, payload hash, precondition, and outcome fields, and close/reopen without losing the next sequence number. Keep the API package-level and production-ready for later wiring.

NON-SCOPE
Do not add server routes, wire Cobra commands, centralize bead picking, or change process ownership. Do not implement the HTTP coordination client. Do not wire Cobra command flows to the journal in this bead beyond package-level production APIs and tests. Do not implement acknowledgement-through resume or compaction in this child.

PARENT
- ddx-635ae251.

DEPS
- No deps.
title: agent: add offline coordination journal append record API

acceptance:
1. TestWorkCleanup_RunsAfterSetupFailureBeforeNextClaim uses an explicit fixture-local TempRoot and ScratchRoots and still proves cleanup runs before the second claim.
2. TestWorkCleanup_RunsAfterSetupFailureBeforeNextClaim_DoesNotTouchSharedScratchRoot installs a sentinel outside the fixture roots and proves Cleanup neither enumerates nor mutates it.
3. TestExecutionCleanupFixtures_UseHermeticRoots audits every internal/agent test that executes a production ExecutionCleanupManager and fails if its effective temp or scratch roots escape t.TempDir.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "Test(WorkCleanup_RunsAfterSetupFailureBeforeNextClaim(_DoesNotTouchSharedScratchRoot)?|ExecutionCleanupFixtures_UseHermeticRoots)$" -count=1 -timeout 30s passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s passes without reading or deleting host-global DDx scratch trees.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The normal internal/agent unit-test gate reads and deletes host-global DDx scratch trees. On 2026-07-13, TestWorkCleanup_RunsAfterSetupFailureBeforeNextClaim drove about 431 MB of physical reads, timed out after 120 seconds in isolation, and caused the required 600-second package gate to time out. This makes test duration depend on accumulated host cache state and adds substantial I/O/CPU pressure while ddx work is trying to drain the queue.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_cleanup_test.go:269-288 creates a production NewExecutionCleanupManager for t.TempDir but does not override TempRoot or ScratchRoots. cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:830-843 therefore derives the shared cache parent and legacy process temp directory. Cleanup reaches cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:620-747, where it recursively measures and removes every old matching scratch directory. The timeout stack was in measureTreeWithContext from cleanupScratchRoots, not in the predispatch fixture under test.

PROPOSED FIX
- Give every execution-cleanup unit fixture an explicit hermetic TempRoot and ScratchRoots beneath its own t.TempDir before calling Cleanup.
- Add a shared test helper if needed so future fixtures cannot silently fall back to ~/.cache/ddx or the process temp root.
- Add a sentinel/tripwire proving the loop-cleanup test cannot enumerate, measure, or remove a scratch root outside its fixture root.
- Audit adjacent NewExecutionCleanupManager test call sites reached by ./internal/agent and apply the same boundary where they invoke Cleanup.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_cleanup_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_test.go and nearest cleanup test helpers as required

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken production cleanup, change retention policy, or skip cleanup assertions. Production cleanup latency is tracked separately.

PARENT
None; root gate-reliability regression.

DEPS
None.

acceptance:
1. TestTrackerLockLeaseReleaseDoesNotDeleteReplacement age-supersedes a live lease, acquires and holds a replacement lease, then releases the old lease and proves token mismatch preserves the replacement pid, acquired_at, and owner_token bytes exactly.
2. withTrackerLockPolicy uses a token-bearing lease and surfaces guarded release errors; withMainGitLock, WithMainGitLock, and withTrackerLock retain their callback signatures.
3. No main-git-lock release path directly removes the canonical pathname without the transition guard and owner_token check.
4. Acquisition writes pid, acquired_at, and owner_token successfully before publishing acquisition; metadata publication failure must fail safe and must not call the protected callback.
5. cd cli && go test -race ./internal/agent/... -run "TestTrackerLockLeaseReleaseDoesNotDeleteReplacement" -count=20 passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
A live holder can be age-superseded by a replacement lock, then later release by pathname and delete the replacement. Acquisition also lacks a strong owner identity and currently ignores metadata publication failures, so release cannot prove whether the canonical lock still belongs to the releasing holder.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:224-230 publishes the canonical lock acquisition before requiring metadata publication success. cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:282 later releases every holder with unconditional RemoveAll. cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:323-328 may classify an over-age lock stale even when its PID is still live, and cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_windows.go:7-16 makes age the only effective stale criterion on Windows. Without a token-bearing lease, an old holder has no way to distinguish its own canonical lock directory from a replacement.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT
Phase 0 WB-5 requires a single-winner cross-process stale-break protocol at docs/helix/06-iterate/phase0-stop-the-self-harm-plan-2026-07-13.md:124-132.

PROPOSED FIX
Internally change main-git lock acquisition to return a lease containing the canonical path and a strong unique owner token while preserving withMainGitLock, WithMainGitLock, and withTrackerLock callback APIs. On successful os.Mkdir, write pid, acquired_at, and owner_token successfully before publishing acquisition. withTrackerLockPolicy must defer lease Release and join any release error with the callback error. Lease release must acquire the transition guard within a bounded policy, re-read canonical owner_token, and remove the canonical directory only when the token exactly matches the lease. Missing or mismatched tokens mean the lease was superseded and release preserves the replacement. If guarded release cannot be proven, return an error and leave the lock in place; never fall back to unconditional RemoveAll.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change tracker mutation semantics, tracker record format, trackerLockStaleAge, LockRetryPolicy defaults, lockmetrics caps, harness/Fizeau behavior, bead-store locks, git index locks, or the separate execution-cleanup lock in cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_loop.go:116-156. Do not extract a shared lock package; ddx-dffb7454 and ddx-3f156500 own separate sites and Phase 1 WB-8 owns unification. Do not implement delayed stale-breaker winner/loser disposal tests beyond the lease-release replacement-preservation case.

PARENT
ddx-8b22914e

DEPS
None. ddx-dffb7454 is a related collection-lock implementation, not a prerequisite or shared-package dependency.
title: agent: make main-git lease release token-safe

acceptance:
1. TestSpechonestyCommandExitsNonZeroOnMissingStatus passes: running the command against a fixture docs tree containing an unstamped SD/TD/ADR document returns a non-zero exit code and includes a missing-status diagnostic.
2. TestSpechonestyCommandExitsZeroForStampedDocs passes: running the command against fixture docs with body and frontmatter statuses returns exit zero.
3. TestSpechonestyCommandIsReadOnly passes: fixture file contents are byte-identical before and after command execution.
4. `cd cli && go test ./tools/lint/spechonesty/...` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The status parser cannot be used as a mechanical gate until there is a `spechonesty` command that accepts a docs directory, scans Markdown documents, reports parse/missing-status failures, and returns an appropriate process exit code without mutating documents.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
WB-1 requires `cd cli && go run ./tools/lint/spechonesty ../docs/helix/` as the validation path and future pre-commit/CI command (`docs/helix/06-iterate/phase2-doc-truth-plan-2026-07-13.md:72`). Existing lint tools expose a command entrypoint through `cmd/<lintname>/main.go`; evidencelint's entrypoint calls its analyzer from `main()` (`cli/tools/lint/evidencelint/cmd/evidencelint/main.go:13`, `cli/tools/lint/evidencelint/cmd/evidencelint/main.go:14`). The new `cli/tools/lint/spechonesty/cmd/spechonesty/main.go` does not exist.

PROPOSED FIX
After the status parser package exists, add `cli/tools/lint/spechonesty/cmd/spechonesty/main.go` and any small package API needed to scan a docs directory recursively for Markdown files. The command must accept one docs directory argument, use the parser from the foundation child, print diagnostics for parse or missing-status failures, exit non-zero when any such failure is found, exit zero otherwise, and never write to the docs tree.

NON-SCOPE
Do not auto-restamp documents, infer proof from prose, fetch network state, or mark aspirational metrics complete. Do not implement verification-coverage, waiver, duplicate-id, or stale-observation logic here. Prose quality and AC numbering style are out of scope. Do not add pre-commit or CI wiring in this child.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT
`docs/helix/06-iterate/phase2-doc-truth-plan-2026-07-13.md` WB-1, especially the new spechonesty package scope at line 63 and command validation requirement at line 72.

PARENT: ddx-af66f903. INHERITED DEPS: none. CHILD DEPENDENCY: depends on the status parser package child from this decomposition.
title: Add spechonesty docs-directory CLI

acceptance:
1. New sentinel `ErrDependencyGateRejected` exists in cli/internal/bead/errors.go and is wrapped (%w) by the gate errors.
2. `Store.UnclosedBlockingDeps` returns the unclosed blocking dep IDs for a target bead and an empty slice when all deps are closed; covered by `TestUnclosedBlockingDeps_ReturnsOpenDepIDs` in cli/internal/bead/store_test.go.
3. `Store.Close` refuses a bead with an open blocks-dep, returning an error satisfying `errors.Is(err, ErrDependencyGateRejected)` whose message contains the open dep ID; the bead stays open. Covered by `TestClose_RefusesOpenBlocksDep`.
4. `Store.CloseWithEvidence` refuses the same case with the same wrapped error and leaves the bead open; covered by `TestCloseWithEvidence_RefusesOpenBlocksDep`.
5. `Store.UpdateWithLifecycleStatus` with status=closed refuses the same case (covering `ddx bead update --status closed`); covered by `TestUpdateWithLifecycleStatus_RefusesCloseWithOpenBlocksDep`.
6. Closing a bead whose blocks-deps are all closed still succeeds (no regression); covered by an assertion in `TestClose_RefusesOpenBlocksDep` or a sibling `TestClose_AllowsClosedBlocksDep`.
7. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/...` green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM: A bead carrying an open 'blocks' dependency can be transitioned to closed. The direct close paths never consult dependency status, so a dependent bead can close while its prerequisite is still in_progress (pqueue-842ac11f closed while blocks-dep pqueue-657e5f8d was in_progress).

ROOT CAUSE: Three distinct operator/CLI close paths all reach StatusClosed without a dependency check. (1) `Store.Close` (cli/internal/bead/store.go:1584) calls `SetLifecycleStatus(id, StatusClosed, ...)` -> `transitionLifecycleInPlace` -> `ValidateLifecycleTransition` (store.go:751; lifecycle.go:103), which validates the single bead only and has no access to sibling dependency statuses. (2) `Store.closeWithEvidence` (store.go:2051) runs `ClosureGate(b)` (store.go:1994) which checks execution evidence but not dependencies. (3) `ddx bead update --status closed` routes through `Store.UpdateWithLifecycleStatus` (store.go:732, called at cmd/bead.go:945) — a materially different path that sets neither ManualClose nor the closure gate and also skips dependency validation. CLI `ddx bead close` maps to Store.Close (cmd/bead.go:1183) and Store.CloseWithEvidence (cmd/bead.go:1186).

PROPOSED FIX: (1) Add sentinel `ErrDependencyGateRejected = errors.New("bead: unclosed blocking dependencies")` in cli/internal/bead/errors.go, mirroring the `ErrClosureGateRejected` pattern (store.go:1976). (2) Add `func (s *Store) UnclosedBlockingDeps(id string) ([]string, error)` that reads all beads, builds a status map keyed by bead ID (same shape as store.go:2288-2292), and reuses the existing pure helper `unclosedDependencyIDs(b, statusMap)` (store.go:2351) over the target bead's `DepIDs()` (cli/internal/bead/types.go:250), returning the sorted dep IDs whose status is not closed (per `LifecycleStatusSatisfiesDependency`, lifecycle.go:139). (3) Before the closed transition in `Store.Close` (store.go:1584) and `Store.closeWithEvidence` (store.go:2051), and in `Store.UpdateWithLifecycleStatus` (store.go:732) guarded by `status == StatusClosed`, call the resolver; if the slice is non-empty return `fmt.Errorf("%w: bead %s: %s", ErrDependencyGateRejected, id, strings.Join(open, ", "))`. The message must name the offending dep IDs.

NON-SCOPE: Do NOT modify the roll-up / cascade auto-close paths (`cascadeAndWalkUp` store.go:1661, `walkUpClosureCandidate` store.go:1712, `cascadeCloseSuperseeded` store.go:1833) — sibling bead. Do NOT modify claim paths (`Store.Claim`, `Store.ClaimWithOptions`) — sibling bead. Do NOT change `ValidateLifecycleTransition` semantics or the pure lifecycle package. Do NOT gate on non-'blocks' dependency types. Do NOT add a --force override flag in this bead.

acceptance:
1. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "Test(ValidateAttemptIntegrity_(MissingRequiredGateEvidenceRejected|NoVerifyImplementationCommitRejected|AllowsDDXOwnedNoVerifyCommits)|ExecuteBead_(WiresHarnessGateEvidenceIntoIntegrityValidation|PromptForbidsNoVerify))$" -count=1 -timeout 120s passes.
2. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s passes.
3. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-9c3e703b is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Attempt 20260713T094206-53684645 for ddx-415c8b95 had a staged pre-commit gate terminated after it recursively walked 100 leaked host-global module caches. The provider then ran git commit --no-verify and DDx accepted, merged, closed, and pushed implementation commit 85bb0fae0. A code-changing attempt can therefore bypass its required staged hook and still be classified as successful.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2108-2113 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2143-2148 require tests and a staged lefthook run but do not explicitly forbid --no-verify, hook disabling, or bypassing a failed gate. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1507-1514 invokes ValidateAttemptIntegrity without populating GateRuns. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_validation.go:160-186 checks gate evidence only when len(GateRuns) is nonzero, and TestValidateAttemptIntegrity_SingleCommitNoEvidenceSkips codifies missing evidence as success. The validation promised by closed bead ddx-725b65b4 is therefore not wired into the live execute-bead path.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add an explicit execute-bead instruction that implementation agents must never use git commit --no-verify, disable hooks, or commit after a required gate failed or was interrupted.
- Capture implementation-agent tool commands and foreground gate outputs/exit codes from the harness session into structured attempt-integrity input.
- When a code-changing bead acceptance requires lefthook run pre-commit, require at least one successful meaningful staged run after the final staged mutation and before the implementation commit. Missing, failed, interrupted, background-only, or no-staged-files evidence must reject and preserve the attempt.
- Treat an implementation-agent git commit --no-verify command as an integrity violation even if a prior gate appeared meaningful.
- Keep DDx-owned checkpoint, tracker-only, landing, and durable-audit commits outside this rule; their scoped --no-verify use is orchestrator-owned.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_validation.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_validation_test.go
- execute-bead prompt guardrail/snapshot tests
- nearest harness-session evidence adapter needed to populate GateRuns and implementation commands

NON-SCOPE
Do not rewrite or remove existing execute-bead history, forbid DDx-owned tracker/aud
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run "Test(ValidateAttemptIntegrity_(MissingRequiredGateEvidenceRejected|NoVerifyImplementationCommitRejected|AllowsDDXOwnedNoVerifyCommits)|ExecuteBead_(WiresHarnessGateEvidenceIntoIntegrityValidation|PromptForbidsNoVerify))$" -count=1 -timeout 120s passes.
- cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -timeout 600s passes.
- lefthook run pre-commit passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
notes: Cancelled as a verification-only decomposition artifact. Its focused, package, and lefthook gates are now acceptance criteria on ddx-c4497391 and ddx-03568f89.
title: agent: reject hook-bypassing implementation commits: part 3

acceptance:
1. TestWorkLoop_PreClaimDecompositionUsesConfiguredPreClaimTimeout injects a blocking hook plus a short configured preclaim timeout and proves DDx returns within the bound, emits a structured timeout observation, and leaves the bead unclaimed.
2. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run TestWorkLoop_PreClaimDecompositionUsesConfiguredPreClaimTimeout -count=1 passes.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... passes.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
While the managed DDx worker decomposed ddx-29491a0e on 2026-07-13, the preclaim-decompose Codex provider remained live for more than two minutes while the worker looked idle. A blocking provider can outlive the configured preclaim guard, delaying queue progress and hiding timeout evidence from operators.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:16 and cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:59-74 give preclaim decomposition a separate fixed eight-minute timeout. This bypasses the configured five-minute preclaim bound from cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go:15 and cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go:95-99. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3108-3120 invokes PostAttemptDecompositionHook before claim without wrapping it in the execute loop's configured preclaim timeout.

PROPOSED FIX
Bound the preclaim decomposition hook by the configured preclaim timeout supplied to the execute loop rather than the longer hard-coded timeout. On timeout, return within the configured bound, emit a structured timeout observation, leave the candidate bead unclaimed, and clear any transient resolving state.

NON-SCOPE

PARENT
Split from root reliability regression bead: work: surface, bound, and reap preclaim decomposition lifecycle.

ddx-aa6cc320 must land first so the internal/agent verification gate does not launch five real Codex providers.

acceptance:
1. TestCoordinationContract_LocalLandingIdempotency verifies the coordination landing contract against the local implementation using a real temporary bead store and git repository.
2. TestCoordinationContract_LocalLandingIdempotency covers landing and idempotent already-applied landing outcomes without call-recording mocks.
3. Production coordination code invoked by the local landing implementation calls the existing real landing path rather than substituting fake recorders.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestCoordinationContract_LocalLandingIdempotency" passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Execution code lacks a transport-neutral coordination domain contract for landing outcomes, so later connected and offline implementations cannot share landing success, already-applied, and failure semantics.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/try.go:256-259 and cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:280-282 construct NewLocalLandCoordinator directly, tying landing behavior to a local concrete path instead of a shared coordination contract. cli/internal/agent/workerprobe/probe.go:129-224 owns only connection state and an in-memory event buffer, not typed landing requests, outcomes, or idempotency semantics.

PROPOSED FIX
Add or extend the coordination domain package under cli/internal/agent with typed landing request/outcome APIs and idempotency key handling. Add the local implementation adapter that invokes the existing real landing path. Add TestCoordinationContract_LocalLandingIdempotency to exercise landing against a real temporary bead store and git repository, including a repeated same-key landing request that returns the contract-defined already-applied outcome without call-recording mocks.

NON-SCOPE
Do not add server routes, wire Cobra commands, centralize bead picking, or change process ownership. Do not implement the HTTP coordination client in this bead. Do not implement the durable offline journal or cross-process offline lock here except for interfaces needed by the contract. Do not change claim picking or tracker transition behavior beyond the minimal setup required for landing.

GOVERNING
ADR-022 rev 6 requires one reconnecting coordination client, durable ordered offline journal, idempotency keys, and a cross-process offline project lock. FEAT-006 Worker Contract requires connected mutations to use the server; disconnected manual workers apply the same operations locally and reconcile before new online writes.

PARENT/DEPS
Parent chain: ddx-3ab6762d; embedded parent reference: ddx-635ae251. DEPS: No deps.
title: agent: define coordination landing contract and local landing test

acceptance:
1. TestOfflineCoordinator_SerializesAcrossProcesses launches two real subprocesses against the same project root and verifies their protected mutation windows do not overlap.
2. TestOfflineCoordinator_SerializesAcrossProcesses fails if the second subprocess enters the protected section before the first releases the project coordination lock.
3. Production offline coordination code uses the same project-scoped lock path exercised by TestOfflineCoordinator_SerializesAcrossProcesses.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestOfflineCoordinator_SerializesAcrossProcesses" passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Spec-first development · Executable specifications · Audit trail required · Work is a DAG · Drift is debt · Inspect and adapt</principles>
<concerns>go-std | security-owasp | testing</concerns>
<practices>Go domain interface with contract tests against real implementations · project-scoped durable files · no call-recording mocks · go test + lefthook</practices>
<adrs>ADR-022 rev 6 requires one reconnecting coordination client, durable ordered offline journal, idempotency keys, and a cross-process offline project lock.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-006 Worker Contract: connected mutations use the server; disconnected manual workers apply the same operations locally and reconcile before new online writes.</governing>
</context-digest>

PROBLEM
Offline/manual coordination has no cross-process project lock, so two CLI processes can overlap the mutation window for claims, tracker transitions, journal writes, or local landing.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/try.go:256-259 and cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:280-282 each construct local coordination directly for the current invocation. cli/internal/agent/workerprobe/probe.go:129-224 tracks connection state only and does not provide a project-wide lock around offline coordination mutations.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a real project-scoped offline coordination lock in cli/internal/agent and apply it to the local/offline coordination mutation window. Cover it with a subprocess-based test that proves two independent processes cannot hold the protected mutation window simultaneously.

NON-SCOPE
Do not add server routes, wire Cobra commands, centralize bead picking, or change process ownership. Do not change the lock lifetime contract for existing git index or tracker locks. Do not implement broad bead picking or worker scheduling changes.

PARENT
- ddx-635ae251.

DEPS
- No deps.

acceptance:
1. `TestLifecycleModelDiscovery_CancellationReapsPTYProbe` uses a hermetic fake probe that changes session/process group like `codex --no-alt-screen`, cancels the caller/lifecycle context, and proves the probe plus descendants are gone before the lifecycle call completes.
2. `TestLifecycleModelDiscovery_ReturnDoesNotLeaveNoAltScreenChild` covers success, upstream error, caller timeout, and worker cancellation and proves no discovery child introduced by the call remains under the worker; a provider process present in the pre-call baseline survives.
3. The selected Fizeau version or patch passes an upstream regression named `TestSubprocessDiscoveryRefresh_CancellationReapsPTYProbe` proving the cache refresher does not discard its context and `ptyquota.Run` is never called with `context.Background()` for lifecycle discovery.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run '^(TestLifecycleModelDiscovery_CancellationReapsPTYProbe|TestLifecycleModelDiscovery_ReturnDoesNotLeaveNoAltScreenChild)$' -count=1 -timeout 60s` passes.
5. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
On Niflheim, the managed DDx worker spawned `codex --no-alt-screen` PID 1760621 during pre-claim route/model discovery. The readiness route resolved and its intake provider exited, but the PTY probe remained a direct worker child for more than 123 seconds, initially consuming about 11% CPU, until the operator killed only its process group. A two-second DDx discovery deadline therefore returns control without stopping the work it started, allowing every queue poll to accumulate provider CPU.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:519-533` gives `svc.ListModels(modelCtx, modelFilter)` a two-second context.
- Fizeau v0.14.50 `internal/serviceimpl/subprocess_discovery_cache.go:74-94` accepts a refresh context but discards it and calls context-free `DefaultModelSnapshot()`; its warm/cold paths at :106-149 can launch refresh in a background goroutine after the caller returns.
- Fizeau v0.14.50 `internal/harnesses/codex/model_discovery.go:20-48` runs `codex --no-alt-screen` through `ptyquota.Run(context.Background(), ...)`, severing both the DDx caller deadline and worker lifecycle cancellation.
- This regresses the containment intent of closed bead `ddx-403e9a23`, whose server-side workaround explicitly left upstream context propagation out of scope.

PROPOSED FIX
Upgrade to a Fizeau release that carries caller/lifecycle context through cached subprocess discovery into `ptyquota.Run`, or patch the dependency first and then update `cli/go.mod` and `cli/go.sum`. An asynchronous cache refresh must own a bounded context, and every success, error, timeout, and cancellation path must wait until the spawned PTY session/process group is gone. Add a DDx call-site regression around lifecycle model discovery. If an upstream release cannot land in the same pass, add a narrowly scoped DDx containment layer that tracks and reaps only discovery descendants created by this call; never scan for or kill unrelated user provider processes.

NON-SCOPE
Disabling model discovery, extending the two-second caller timeout, killing manual Codex sessions or provider processes that predate the call, changing route selection, or restarting the DDx server.

PARENT
None; root reliability/performance regression observed while draining the Niflheim queue.

DEPS
Related to `ddx-092f0d34` (preclaim lifecycle containment) and regression of `ddx-403e9a23`; neither relation permits killing processes outside the current worker/call baseline.

2. TestExecutionCleanup_ReclaimsMetadataMarkedHostGlobalScratch proves an old host-global candidate with cleanup.json for the current project still passes liveness checks and is reclaimed.

description:
PROBLEM
A worker cleanup pass treats an old directory name as sufficient proof that the current project owns the directory. During the 2026-07-13 Niflheim gate, cleanup entered /home/erik/.cache/fleet-tmp/ddx-home-3567057526, a metadata-less test HOME containing a 468 MB Go module cache. The race-test binary spent minutes traversing that unrelated tree and its Go testlog grew past 2.7 GB. A prefix collision or another project test fixture can therefore trigger cross-project traversal and deletion.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:667-681 handles a missing cleanup.json by synthesizing ExecutionCleanupMetadata with ProjectRoot set to the manager current project and WorktreePath set to the discovered path. cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:738-755 then recursively measures and removes the tree. cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:829-844 always includes os.TempDir as a legacy scratch root. Prefixes such as ddx-home- at cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:25-45 are a discovery hint, not durable ownership or liveness evidence.

PROPOSED FIX
Classify scratch-root trust before reclamation. For a host-global or legacy process temp root, preserve a matching directory whose cleanup metadata is missing instead of synthesizing current-project ownership, do not call measureTreeWithContext or removal for it, and emit a structured unowned-scratch observation. Continue reclaiming metadata-marked paths after existing liveness and project checks. Permit metadata-less reclamation only inside an explicitly configured project-private scratch boundary whose containment itself establishes ownership. Update legacy cleanup fixtures so they distinguish discovery prefixes from ownership evidence.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_test.go
- nearest cleanup summary/observation tests

NON-SCOPE
Do not add cleanup time budgets; ddx-de1ff4b4 owns bounded reclamation. Do not migrate scratch producers; ddx-6a2ce3bd owns producer placement. Do not disable cleanup of metadata-marked stale paths or weaken active-worktree preservation.

PARENT
None; root cleanup ownership regression.

DEPS
None.

acceptance:
1. TestOperation_QueueSetPositionCanonicalizesLegacyAlias proves QueueSetPosition replaces a legacy queue_rank with one numeric queue-rank and leaves no alias.
2. TestOpsQueue_ClearRemovesLegacyAliasWithoutResurrection seeds an alias-only ranked bead, clears it, and proves bead.QueueRank reports no rank and natural bucket ordering is restored.
3. TestBeadQueueTopThenClearDoesNotRestoreLegacyRank drives the real queue top and queue clear commands against a legacy row and proves the old rank cannot reappear after the canonical override is cleared.
4. Existing TestQueueRankUnderscoreAliasCompatibility remains green and proves read compatibility is preserved.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/bead/ops/queue/... ./cmd/... -run "^(TestOperation_QueueSetPositionCanonicalizesLegacyAlias|TestOpsQueue_ClearRemovesLegacyAliasWithoutResurrection|TestBeadQueueTopThenClearDoesNotRestoreLegacyRank|TestQueueRankUnderscoreAliasCompatibility)$" -count=1 passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/... passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Legacy tracker rows can carry queue_rank instead of queue-rank. A queue move/top writes a new canonical rank without removing that alias, and queue clear removes only the canonical key. Clearing can therefore resurrect the stale legacy rank and silently restore an ordering the operator explicitly removed.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/queue.go:20-29 defines actual queue ordering so canonical queue-rank wins while present, then falls back to legacy queue_rank.
- cli/internal/bead/operation.go:12-31 QueueSetTop and QueueSetPosition write only queue-rank and leave queue_rank behind.
- cli/internal/bead/operation.go:38-45 QueueClearOp deletes only queue-rank, allowing a hidden queue_rank value to become authoritative again.
- cli/internal/bead/queue.go:49-64 already defines the correct write-time canonicalization behavior for generic Extra mutations, but queue operations do not share it.

PROPOSED FIX
Make queue rank operations canonicalize on write by deleting queue_rank whenever QueueSetTop or QueueSetPosition persists queue-rank. Make QueueClearOp remove both accepted spellings. Add operation-, ops-, and command-boundary tests proving a legacy rank cannot survive or resurrect after top/move/clear.

NON-SCOPE
Do not remove read compatibility for legacy queue_rank rows, rewrite the tracker in bulk, change priority or ready eligibility, alter queue ordering semantics, or hand-edit beads.jsonl.

PARENT
None; root compatibility follow-up discovered while reviewing ddx-87ed3efe.

DEPS
Depends on ddx-87ed3efe because both beads touch queue operations and its Top implementation establishes the current ordering path.

acceptance:
1. TestValidationSupervisor_OutlivesHarnessToolCallCeiling uses a short fake harness-tool ceiling and a longer real helper, proves the tool caller can disconnect, then reattaches and receives one persisted stdout/stderr stream and the real exit 0 without relaunching the helper.
2. TestValidationSupervisor_SameFingerprintReattaches proves direct, timeout-wrapped, redirected, nohup, and start_new_session requests with the same normalized command/cwd/env/worktree fingerprint return the existing validation ID and exactly one command process.
3. TestValidationSupervisor_ChangedFingerprintStartsOneNewRun proves a tracked-worktree or relevant config mutation permits one new supervised validation and records why prior evidence is invalid.
4. TestValidationSupervisor_CancelReapsSessionChangingDescendants proves attempt cancellation and server shutdown remove the command plus setsid/reparented descendants, persist a non-success result, and emit operator_attention with attempt, bead, validation ID, fingerprint, and last activity.
5. TestValidationSupervisor_ManualManagedParity proves manual/offline and server-managed workers expose the same start/status/await and reconnect behavior; the managed command remains in ddx-server.service with KillMode=control-group semantics.
6. TestExecuteBeadLongRunningMatrixPromptSnapshotValidation is replaced or extended by behavioral coverage proving a declared 30-minute gate is not constrained by a shorter harness tool-call ceiling and cannot launch an untracked detached fallback.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... ./cmd/... -run "^(TestValidationSupervisor_(OutlivesHarnessToolCallCeiling|SameFingerprintReattaches|ChangedFingerprintStartsOneNewRun|CancelReapsSessionChangingDescendants|ManualManagedParity)|TestExecuteBeadLongRunningMatrixPromptSnapshotValidation)$" -count=1 -timeout 180s passes.
8. cd cli && go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./internal/agent ./internal/server ./cmd passes.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes on the final staged tree.
description:
PROBLEM
A bead can require a validation timeout longer than the harness shell tool permits. In ddx-5653688f attempt 20260713T195457-44c4a97d, cd cli && go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./internal/agent was still making progress with one bounded test process when the Codex bash tool forcibly terminated it at 20m20.150s with exit 143. The model then tried the same command through background shell, nohup, and Python start_new_session wrappers to evade the tool ceiling. The first two died with their tool shells; the third survived by reparenting to the user systemd manager inside ddx-server.service, leaving DDx unable to attach an exit status or automatically associate that process with the attempt. A valid 30-minute acceptance gate therefore cannot complete through the normal path and provokes exactly the detached replay behavior DDx forbids.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2085-2094 expresses long-command safety only as prompt text. It says to stop after incomplete evidence but provides no DDx-owned way to run a command beyond a harness tool ceiling.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_long_running_fixture_test.go:102-145 explicitly calls prompt-only enforcement sufficient and tests only that phrases exist, not that a long gate can finish once or that detached recovery is contained.
- cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:25-35 observes a tool call only until its result/deadline; it does not own a separately addressable validation subprocess that a later tool call can reattach to.
- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:10-39 contains only the harness process group. A command intentionally launched with start_new_session can leave harness ancestry even though it remains in the aggregate server cgroup.

PROPOSED FIX
Create one attempt-owned validation supervisor before the model harness starts and expose a small start/status/await client surface to the model. A long command must be registered by normalized command, cwd, environment/config, and tracked-worktree fingerprint; the supervisor launches it inside the attempt controller, persists stdout/stderr and the real exit status under the execution bundle, and continues when an individual harness tool call reaches its ceiling. Reissuing start for the same fingerprint must reattach to the existing run rather than launch a duplicate. Cancellation, attempt exit, or server death must reap the supervised command and session-changing descendants. The same supervisor/client contract must work for manual/offline and server-managed workers; managed descendants remain in ddx-server.service and die with the service. Replace prompt-only snapshot confidence with behavioral integration coverage.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not infer success from exit 143, missing exit evidence, a PID-only file, or a killed command.
- Do not permit arbitrary detached daemons outside an attempt controller.
- Do not weaken project-declared validation timeouts or skip long tests; ddx-85d27282 separately removes a chaos matrix from short mode.
- Do not duplicate semantic replay detection owned by parent ddx-7fb837a0 or the CPU envelope owned by ddx-7df6b12a.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent ddx-7fb837a0. Depends on ddx-7df6b12a for attempt-controller containment and cleanup.

title: agent: supervise long validation outside harness tool-call ceilings
